program_outcomes: Matematik, fen bilimlerine özgü konularda yeterli bilgi birikimine sahiptir.
Yazılım mühendisliğine özgü konularda yeterli bilgi birikimine sahiptir.
Matematik, fen bilimleri ve yazılım mühendisliğine özgü konulardaki kuramsal ve uygulamalı bilgileri, karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin çözümünde kullanabilme becerisine sahiptir.
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme becerisi ve bu amaçla uygun analiz ve modelleme yöntemlerini seçme ve uygulama becerisine sahiptir.

Karmaşık bir sistemi, süreci, cihazı veya ürünü gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında, belirli gereksinimleri karşılayacak şekilde tasarlama becerisine ve bu amaçla modern tasarım yöntemlerini uygulama becerisine sahiptir.

Mühendislik uygulamalarında karşılaşılan karmaşık problemlerin analizi ve çözümü için gerekli olan modern teknik ve araçları seçme ve kullanma becerisi ile bilişim teknolojilerini etkin bir şekilde kullanma becerisine sahiptir.

Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin veya yazılım mühendisliğine özgü araştırma konularının incelenmesi için deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisine sahiptir.
Disiplin içi takımlarda etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisine sahiptir.
Çok disiplinli takımlarda etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisine sahiptir.

Sözlü ve yazılı etkin iletişim kurma becerisi; en az bir yabancı dil bilgisi; etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme, etkin sunum yapabilme, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisine sahiptir.
Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi gibi, iş hayatındaki uygulamalar hakkında bilgiye sahiptir.
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği konusunda farkındalık; bilgiye erişebilme, bilim ve teknolojideki gelişmeleri izleme ve kendini sürekli yenileme becerisine sahiptir.
Etik ilkelerine uygun davranma, mesleki ve etik sorumluluk ve mühendislik uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgiye sahiptir.
Mühendislik uygulamalarının evrensel ve toplumsal boyutlarda sağlık, çevre ve güvenlik üzerindeki etkileri ve çağın mühendislik alanına yansıyan sorunları hakkında bilgiye sahiptir.
Mühendislik çözümlerinin hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalığa sahiptir.

courses.name: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılap Tarihi 1
courses.purpose: Türk Bağımsızlık Savaşı öncesi dönemi kavratarak Atatürk İnkılâpları ve İlkeleri, Atatürkçü düşünce ve Türkiye Cumhuriyeti tarihi hakkında doğru bilgi vermek; Türkiye ve Atatürk İnkılâpları ile İlkelerine yönelik tehditler konusunda bilinç kazandırmak; öğrencileri Atatürkçü düşünce doğrultusunda yetiştirip güçlendirmek.
courses.description: Osmanlı İmparatorluğu'nun dağılması, Tanzimat ve Islahat Fermanı, I. ve II. Meşrutiyet Dönemi, Trablusgarp ve Balkan Savaşları, I. Dünya Savaşı, Mondros Ateşkes Antlaşması, Kurtuluş Savaşı, Amasya Genelgesi, ulusal kongreler, TBMM'nin kuruluşu ve Cumhuriyetin ilanı.
learning_outcomes: Atatürk İlkeleri konusunda bilinçli ve İnkılâp Tarihi konusunda bilgili olmak.
Osmanlı İmparatorluğu'nun yıkılışını ve Türk İnkılâbını hazırlayan nedenleri açıklayabilmek.
Osmanlı İmparatorluğu'nda yapılan yenileşme hareketlerini açıklayabilmek.
Osmanlı Devleti'nin parçalanmasına neden olan Balkan ve Birinci Dünya Savaşlarının sebep ve sonuçlarını açıklayabilmek.
İşgallere karşı tepkileri açıklayabilmek.
TBMM’nin açılması ve cumhuriyetin kuruluşunu açıklamak.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: I. Meşrutiyet Dönemi. II. Abdülhamid, Genç Osmanlılar
Hafta 4: II. Abdülhamid İstibdadı ve Genç Türkler
Hafta 11: Mondros Mütarekesi, Amasya Genelgesi, Kongreler

Osmanlı İmparatorluğu ve Modern Türkiye II, Stanford J. Shaw & Ezel Kural Shaw
Emergence of Modern Turkey, Bernard Lewis
Ottoman Empire and Modern Turkey II, Stanford J. Shaw & Ezel Kural Shaw

courses.name: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılap Tarihi 2
courses.purpose: Türk Bağımsızlık Savaşı, Atatürk İnkılâpları ve İlkeleri, Atatürkçü düşünce ve Türkiye Cumhuriyeti tarihi hakkında doğru bilgiler vermek; Atatürk İnkılâpları ve İlkeleri ile Atatürkçü düşünceye yönelik tehditler konusunda farkındalık kazandırmak. Türk gençliğini ülkesine, milletine ve devletine bağlı, Atatürkçü düşünce doğrultusunda ulusal hedefler etrafında birleşmiş bireyler olarak yetiştirmek.
courses.description: Siyasi alanda yapılan devrimler, siyasi partiler ve çok partili siyasi hayata geçiş denemeleri; hukuk alanında yapılan devrimler; toplumsal yaşayışın düzenlenmesi; ekonomik alanda yapılan yenilikler; 1923-1938 döneminde Türk dış politikası ve Atatürk sonrası Türk dış politikası.
learning_outcomes: Kurtuluş Savaşı döneminde Anadolu’yu açıklayabilmek
İstiklal Harbi’nde çıkan isyanları açıklayabilmek
Kurtuluş Savaşı’ndaki askeri gelişmeleri kavrayabilmek
Kurtuluş Savaşı’ndaki diplomatik gelişmeleri değerlendirebilmek
İnkılâpların hedefini ve çeşitli alanlarda yapılan inkılâpları açıklayabilmek
Atatürk döneminde dış politikayı açıklayabilmek
Atatürk ilkelerini kavrayabilmek
Temel ve bütünleyici ilkeleri açıklayabilmek
Atatürk sonrası meydana gelen önemli iç ve dış olayları açıklayabilmek
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Kurtuluş Savaşında Diplomatik Gelişmeler, Gümrü Antlaşması, Türk-Sovyet İlişkileri, Türk-Afgan İlişkileri, Londra Konferansı, Türk-İngiliz İlişkileri
Hafta 4: Kurtuluş Savaşında Diplomatik Gelişmeler, Türk-Fransız Görüşmeleri ve Ankara Antlaşması, Türk-İtalyan İlişkileri, Mudanya Mütarekesi, Lozan Antlaşması
Hafta 5: Atatürk Dönemi, İnkılâpların hedefi, Siyasal alanda yapılan inkılâplar
Hafta 6: Atatürk Dönemi Hukuk alanında yapılan inkılâplar
Hafta 7: Atatürk Dönemi Eğitim ve Kültür alanında yapılan inkılâplar, Toplumsal yaşayışın düzenlenmesi ile ilgili inkılâplar

Hafta 15: Atatürk sonrası dönem (1938-1960 yılları arası)

courses.name: Advanced Programming with Python
courses.purpose: This course introduces Python to students with no prior programming experience through a four-stage project progression from file system automation to data analysis. It emphasizes when to apply different approaches based on problem complexity, scalability needs, and resource constraints, including files versus databases for storage and APIs versus scraping for data collection.
courses.description: Variables and data types, conditional statements, loops, lists and collections, functions and modular design, file I/O operations, file system manipulation, error handling with try-except, HTTP requests and API integration, JSON parsing, web scraping with BeautifulSoup, regular expressions, task scheduling and automation, relational databases and SQL, Python-MySQL integration, CRUD operations, database schema design, command-line interfaces, NumPy arrays, pandas DataFrames, data cleaning and preprocessing, matplotlib visualization, grouping and merging, statistical analysis.
learning_outcomes: Apply fundamental programming principles including data structures, control flow, functions, and algorithms to define, formulate, and solve computational problems, automate real-world tasks, and demonstrate effective use of modern development tools and programming techniques.

Design and develop software systems that interact with external data sources through APIs and web scraping, manage state and scheduling for automated monitoring, and operate under realistic constraints such as network reliability, rate limits, and error handling while meeting specified functional requirements.

Work effectively in multidisciplinary teams to develop database-backed applications using relational database design principles and secure CRUD operations, while applying project management skills to coordinate work distribution, meet deadlines, manage risks, and deliver functional systems with professional documentation and version control within three-week development cycles.

Plan and conduct data analysis experiments by collecting data from multiple sources, cleaning and preprocessing real-world datasets, performing statistical analyses using modern analytical tools, and communicating findings effectively through professional visualizations, written reports, and oral presentations for technical and non-technical audiences.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Lists, Loops and File Operations
Hafta 3: Functions and Advanced File Operations
Hafta 9: Business Logic and User Interfaces
resources: https://www.coursera.org/specializations/python

courses.name: Analysis of Algorithms
courses.purpose: This course teaches students how to evaluate the efficiency and performance of algorithms. Students learn various techniques for analyzing algorithms, such as time complexity, space complexity, and asymptotic analysis, and how to choose the most appropriate approach for solving a given problem.
courses.description: The course covers different types of algorithms such as sorting, searching, and graph algorithms, as well as common data structures used in algorithm design. Students also gain knowledge about algorithmic strategies and techniques such as divide and conquer, decrease and conquer, transform and conquer, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, and backtracking algorithms.
learning_outcomes: Evaluate the efficiency and performance of different algorithms using techniques such as time complexity, space complexity, and asymptotic analysis.

Apply various algorithmic strategies and techniques such as divide and conquer, decrease and conquer, transform and conquer, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, and backtracking algorithms to solve different types of problems.
Implement algorithms using common data structures such as arrays, linked lists, trees, and hash tables.
Analyze the performance of algorithmic solutions using experimental methods and theoretical analysis.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Graph Representations and Traversal Algorithms
Hafta 5: Mathematical Induction and Basic Algorithm Design with Induction
Hafta 6: Foundations of Divide and Conquer: Paradigm, Recursive Structure, and Basic Examples
Hafta 13: Graph Applications of Greedy Algorithms
resources: Cormen, Thomas H., Charles E. Leiserson, Ronald L. Rivest, and Clifford Stein. Introduction to algorithms. MIT press, 2022.
Lecture notes

courses.name: Machine Learning
courses.purpose: This course covers the fundamentals of supervised and unsupervised learning, classification, regression, clustering, decision trees, and deep learning. Students will learn how to apply these techniques to solve real-world problems such as image classification, natural language processing, and recommendation systems. The course also addresses model evaluation, feature selection, and data preprocessing, which are essential for developing effective machine learning models.
courses.description: The course content includes linear regression, logistic regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Support Vector Machines, Naive Bayes, K-means clustering, Principal Component Analysis, ensemble learning algorithms such as random forests and gradient boosting machines, and artificial neural networks with single-layer and multilayer perceptrons.
learning_outcomes: Understand the principles and techniques of supervised and unsupervised learning, classification, regression, clustering, decision trees, and deep learning.
Apply machine learning techniques to solve real-world problems such as image classification, natural language processing, and recommendation systems.
Evaluate and select appropriate models for given problem situations using model evaluation and feature selection concepts.
Preprocess data for machine learning modeling.
Analyze and interpret machine learning model outputs to gain insights and make informed decisions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Supervised Learning - Classification Problem - Logistic Regression - K-Nearest Neighbors
Hafta 4: Exploratory Data Analysis - Data Preprocessing
Hafta 5: Supervised Learning - Decision Tree - Handling Imbalanced Datasets
Hafta 6: Supervised Learning - Random Forest - Cross Validation and Its Types
Hafta 7: Supervised Learning - Naive Bayes and Support Vector Machines
Hafta 9: Supervised Learning - Hyperparameter Tuning - Performance Metrics
Hafta 14: Artificial Neural Networks - Multilayer Networks
resources: Introduction to Machine Learning, Ethem Alpaydın (3rd Edition), 2014, MIT Press
Understanding Machine Learning: From Theory to Algorithms, Shai Shalev-Shwartz, Shai Ben-David (1st Edition), 2014, Cambridge University Press
Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning, Christopher Bishop (1st Edition), 2006, Springer
Learning from Data, Yaser S. Abu-Mostafa, Malik Magdon-Ismail, Hsuan-Tien Lin (1st Edition), 2012, AMLBook
Linear Algebra and Optimization for Machine Learning: A Textbook, Charu C. Aggarwal (1st Edition), 2020, Springer Press
Optimization for Machine Learning, Suvrit Sra, Sebastian Nowozin, Stephen J. Wright (Edited - 1st Edition), 2011, MIT Press

courses.name: Database Management Systems
courses.purpose: To design and implement a basic database system using a database management system (DBMS). The course also aims to teach students how to represent data entities and relationships with ER diagrams, apply normalization techniques, write SQL queries, manage transactions, and improve database performance through indexing and query optimization.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts and skills required to manage data effectively using a database management system (DBMS). Students will learn about the entity-relationship (ER) model, relational model and data definition language, functional dependencies, SQL, transactions, concurrency control, file and disk management, recovery systems, indexing, and query processing and optimization. They will also learn how to improve database performance through normalization, indexing, and query optimization techniques.
learning_outcomes: Apply database concepts, entities, tables, and fields
Model data and design a structured database
Design and implement efficient queries using SQL
Evaluate and improve database security and connect it with software applications
Normalize databases and improve database performance
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Relational Data Model - Relational Model
Hafta 3: Relational Data Model - Relational Algebra
Hafta 4: Database Design - ER Data Model
Hafta 5: Database Design - ER Data Model
Hafta 6: SQL - Data Definition Language, Data Manipulation Language
Hafta 7: SQL - Query Language - Set Operations
Hafta 9: SQL - Subqueries - Set membership test, set comparison, empty set test
Hafta 11: SQL - Constraints: data type, primary key, foreign key, not null, column constraints
resources: Database System Concepts by Abraham Silberschatz, Henry F. Korth, and S. Sudarshan
ders notları

courses.name: Occupational Health and Safety 1
courses.purpose: To provide students with general and up-to-date knowledge about occupational health and safety and to establish a relationship between engineering and occupational health and safety.
courses.description: Occupational health and safety concept, its history, laws, practices, occupational diseases, risk analysis, quality systems.
learning_outcomes: Students will learn the basic concepts related to occupational safety and worker health.
Know the causes of work accidents and occupational diseases and the precautions to be taken.
Students will adopt risk, prevention and safety culture.
Learn the causes of work accidents in the machinery industry and the measures to be taken.
Students will understand the responsibilities of the engineer in terms of occupational safety.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course, Occupational Health and Safety concept and the history of OHS
Hafta 2: Labor law, occupational safety, legal definitions, regulations
Hafta 3: Occupational Health and Safety training in Industrial Engineering
Hafta 4: Occupational Health and Safety management systems
Hafta 5: Occupational Health and Safety management systems
Hafta 6: Physical factors, chemical and biological factors
Hafta 7: Physical factors, chemical and biological factors
Hafta 9: Psychological factors, occupational diseases, anthropometry
resources: Lecture notes
The Orange Book, Management of Risk Principles and Concepts, October 2004, HM Treasury, United Kingdom (UK)
ANSI, ANSI/ISA S84.01 and Draft IEC 61508 (1998). Safety Integrity Level - How This Standard Will Affect Your Business
Camerun, I., Raman, R. (2005). Process Systems Risk Management, Elsevier
Moosa, Imad A. (2007). Operational Risk: A Survey. Financial Markets, Institutions & Instruments, Vol. 16, No. 4, pp. 167-200

courses.name: Occupational Health and Safety 2
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide the necessary information to ensure employees' occupational safety and protect their health.
courses.description: The course covers the rules of occupational health and safety, and teaches the importance of first aid and safety precautions.
learning_outcomes: Knows standard safety, health, and occupational safety law.
Has knowledge of the basic rules and current regulations related to occupational health and safety.
Understands occupational health.
Understands safety regulations.
Can apply safety regulations.
Knows safety measures that can be taken in the workplace.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Regulations for health and safety
Hafta 4: Human and vehicle movements - hazard and control
Hafta 5: Manual and mechanical handling hazard and control
Hafta 8: Occupational health and safety rules
Hafta 9: Regulation on Occupational Health and Safety Committees
Hafta 10: Regulation on the Use of Personal Protective Equipment in Workplaces
Hafta 11: Safety precautions for repair and maintenance
Hafta 12: Safety precautions to be taken for machine lifting
Hafta 13: Regulation on Health and Safety Conditions in the Use of Work Equipment

The Orange Book, Management of Risk Principles and Concepts, October 2004, HM Treasury, United Kingdom (UK).

courses.name: Calculus 1
courses.purpose: To teach the concepts of limit, continuity, derivative, and integral in single-variable functions and to develop the ability to use these concepts in solving engineering problems.
courses.description: Functions, graphs, limits, continuity, definition of derivative, differentiation rules, chain rule, differentiation of implicit functions, applications of derivatives, definite integral, indefinite integral, applications of integrals, and transcendental functions.
learning_outcomes: Understands the concept of functions, some basic functions, and their properties.
Understands the concepts of limit and continuity analytically and geometrically; can compute limits for some basic functions.
Understands the concept of derivative together with its geometric meaning, computes derivatives using basic differentiation rules, and applies derivatives to related problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Real numbers, real number line, some basic functions and their graphs, trigonometric functions, composition of functions
Hafta 2: Limits of functions and limit rules, one-sided limits
Hafta 3: Limits at infinity and infinite limits, continuity, intermediate value theorem
Hafta 4: Tangent lines, derivative of a function, differentials
Hafta 6: Derivatives of trigonometric functions, higher-order derivatives
Hafta 7: Inverse functions, exponential and logarithmic functions
Hafta 8: Inverse trigonometric functions, L’Hospital’s rule
Hafta 10: Implicit differentiation, mean value theorem and applications
Hafta 11: Monotonic functions, extreme values, concavity
resources: Thomas, G.B. et al., Thomas’ Calculus, Pearson
Lecture Notes

courses.name: Calculus 2
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide in-depth knowledge of the computation of definite and indefinite integrals, applications of integrals, differentiation of multivariable functions, optimization, and integration of multivariable functions.
courses.description: The course covers definite and indefinite integrals, applications of integrals, multivariable functions, differentiation and optimization of multivariable functions, and integration of multivariable functions. It also includes the computation of double integrals in Cartesian and polar coordinate systems.
learning_outcomes: Understand the concept of the integral together with its geometric meaning, compute definite and indefinite integrals using the main integration techniques, and use integrals in solving various problems.

Understand the concept of multivariable functions, compute partial and directional derivatives, write equations of tangent planes to surfaces, and apply partial derivatives to find and test local extrema.
Compute double integrals in Cartesian and polar coordinates and triple integrals in Cartesian coordinates.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Antiderivatives, sums, sigma notation, areas as limits of sums, definite integral
Hafta 2: Some properties of the definite integral, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, areas between curves
Hafta 3: Some integration techniques: substitution method, integration by parts
Hafta 4: Trigonometric integrals, integrals of rational functions
Hafta 5: Applications of the definite integral: volumes using cross sections and volumes using cylindrical shells
Hafta 6: Vectors and geometry of three-dimensional space
Hafta 7: Vector functions, curves and parametrizations
Hafta 8: Multivariable functions, limits and continuity in higher dimensions
Hafta 10: Directional derivatives and gradient vectors, tangent planes
Hafta 11: Local extrema, saddle points and Lagrange multipliers
resources: Thomas’ Calculus
Calculus, a complete course - Robert Adams, Christopher Essex

courses.name: Discrete Mathematics
courses.purpose: This course introduces discrete mathematical structures and methods such as formal mathematical reasoning, algorithm formulation, time complexity analysis, basic counting techniques, relations, graphs, and trees. It aims to provide the mathematical foundation needed for computation-intensive fields such as computer science and to apply these skills to practical problems.
courses.description: The course covers propositional logic, proof methods, sets, functions, sequences, sums, algorithms, growth of functions, algorithm complexity, basic number theory, cryptography, counting, solving recurrence relations, relations, graphs, and trees.
learning_outcomes: Express mathematical arguments and natural language statements using symbolic logic, determine the validity of arguments using logic and inference rules, and perform simple mathematical proofs.
Write computer programs in formal mathematical language using pseudocode and analyze algorithms in terms of time complexity.
Understand concepts of basic number theory such as modular arithmetic, integer representations, and primality, and their basic applications in cryptography.
Understand and apply counting principles.
Solve recurrence relations.
Know the basic properties of relations, graphs, and trees.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Propositional logic and its applications; propositional functions and quantifiers
Hafta 2: Rules of inference, proof methods
Hafta 3: Sets, functions, sequences, and sums
Hafta 5: Growth of functions, algorithm complexity
Hafta 6: Divisibility, modular arithmetic, integer representations
Hafta 7: Prime numbers, greatest common divisor
Hafta 10: Mathematical induction, strong induction, and well-ordering
Hafta 12: Recurrence relations and their solution
resources: Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications, Kenneth H. Rosen, McGraw-Hill Education
Discrete Mathematics, Richard Johnsonbaugh, Pearson

courses.name: Linear Algebra with Applications
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the fundamental concepts of linear algebra, develop their abstract thinking skills, and enable them to use these concepts in solving engineering problems.
courses.description: Linear equation systems and their solution sets, linear transformations, matrices and matrix operations, determinants, vector spaces, subspaces, linear independence, dimension, basis, change of basis, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, inner product, orthogonality, and singular value decomposition.
learning_outcomes: Solve linear equation systems using elimination methods.

Represent linear transformations with matrices and write matrix representations of linear transformations; perform basic arithmetic operations with matrices, find the inverse of an invertible matrix, and perform matrix decomposition.
Calculate the determinant of a matrix and know the properties of determinants.
Find the dimension and basis of a vector space and its subspaces, and analyze some fundamental subspaces.

Find the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a matrix using the characteristic equation, determine whether a given matrix is diagonalizable, and know how symmetric matrices can be diagonalized and singular value decomposition.
Understand the concepts of length, distance, and orthogonality in inner product spaces, and find an orthogonal basis for any subspace.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Linear equation systems, reduced row and echelon forms
Hafta 2: Vector equations, Ax=b matrix equation, solution sets of linear systems, linear independence
Hafta 3: Introduction to linear transformations, matrix of a linear transformation
Hafta 4: Matrix operations, inverse of a matrix, characterization of invertible matrices
Hafta 5: Block matrices, LU decomposition method
Hafta 6: Determinants, properties of determinants, Cramer's rule, volume calculation
Hafta 7: Vector spaces, subspaces, zero space and column space
Hafta 8: Kernel and image of linear transformations
Hafta 9: Linearly independent sets, spanning, bases, coordinates
Hafta 10: Dimension, rank, change of basis
Hafta 11: Eigenvalues and eigenvectors, characteristic equation, diagonalization
Hafta 12: Inner product spaces, length, distance, orthogonality, orthogonal sets
Hafta 13: Orthogonal projection, Gram-Schmidt process and QR decomposition method
Hafta 14: Diagonalization of symmetric matrices, singular value decomposition
resources: Linear Algebra and Its Applications, David C. Lay, Steven R. Lay, Judi J. McDonald, Pearson.
Elementary Linear Algebra, Howard Anton, Chris Rorres, Wiley, 11th Edition.

courses.name: Differential Equations
courses.purpose: To teach students the fundamental concepts of ordinary differential equations and solution methods, and to equip them with the ability to use these concepts in solving engineering problems.
courses.description: Introduction to ordinary differential equations, first-order differential equations, second-order linear equations, higher-order linear equations, series solutions of second-order linear equations, Laplace transform, and systems of first-order linear equations.
learning_outcomes: Solve separable and linear first-order differential equations.
Find the fundamental solution and general solution of certain second-order linear equations.
Use the Laplace transform method to solve linear ordinary differential equations.
Find particular solutions of nonhomogeneous linear systems of ordinary differential equations.
Solve certain higher-order linear differential equations and differential equation systems.
Apply mathematical modeling in fields such as physics, engineering, biology, or economics.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, classification of differential equations
Hafta 2: First-order differential equations: linear equations, integrating factor method, separable equations, difference between linear and nonlinear equations
Hafta 3: Exact equations and integrating factors, existence and uniqueness theorem
Hafta 4: Second-order linear equations: homogeneous equations with constant coefficients, fundamental solutions of linear homogeneous equations, linear independence, Wronskian
Hafta 5: Complex roots, reduction of order method for repeated roots
Hafta 6: Nonhomogeneous equations: method of undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters
Hafta 7: Higher-order linear equations: general theory, homogeneous equations with constant coefficients, method of undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters
Hafta 9: Laplace transform: definitions, solving initial value problems
Hafta 10: Step functions, solving differential equations with discontinuous forcing functions
Hafta 12: First-order linear systems: introduction, linear independence, eigenvalues, eigenvectors
Hafta 13: Complex eigenvalues, fundamental matrices, repeated eigenvalues, nonhomogeneous linear systems
Hafta 14: Series solutions: power series, series solutions near ordinary points
resources: Boyce, William E.; DiPrima, Richard C., Meade, Douglas B., Elementary Differential Equations and Boundary Value Problems, 12th Edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2021.
Lecture notes

courses.name: Probability and Statistics
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the concepts and ideas related to statistics and probability, and to help students establish meaningful relationships among these concepts and ideas. It also aims to develop statistical thinking and reasoning skills.
courses.description: The course covers sample space, probability, conditional probability, counting, combinatorics, discrete/continuous random variables, conditioning, independence, expectation, variance, covariance, Bayesian inference, sampling distributions, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, and linear regression.
learning_outcomes: Understand and apply the basic concepts of probability (sample spaces, counting, etc.), the mathematical definitions of random variables, and distribution functions.
Comprehend commonly used random variables such as Uniform, Gaussian, and Poisson.
Calculate moments of random variables, including mean and variance.
Characterize multiple random variables using joint distribution functions.
Understand the law of large numbers and the central limit theorem.
Use statistical concepts and tools to interpret engineering data.
resources: Walpole, M. (2016). Probability and Statistics for Engineers and Scientists (9th/Global edition), Pearson Education.
Baron, M. (2014/2019). Probability and Statistics for Computer Scientists (2nd or 3rd edition), CRC Press / Taylor & Francis.
Richard A. Johnson. Probability and Statistics for Engineers (Ninth/Global Edition), Pearson.

courses.name: Numerical Methods for Engineers
courses.purpose: To teach students the application of numerical methods in computer-aided solutions of problems arising in engineering design and analysis.
courses.description: Modeling, computers and error analysis, error propagation, loss of significance, stability, condition number, roots of nonlinear equations, numerical solutions of linear equation systems (direct/iterative), interpolation (Vandermonde, divided differences, spline), curve fitting (linear and nonlinear regression), numerical differentiation, numerical integration, numerical solutions of differential equations, and an overview of advanced topics in numerical analysis.
learning_outcomes: Can perform coding of real numbers in a basic computer environment, function approximation (Taylor), condition number, truncation and error analysis.
Can solve real roots of nonlinear functions or linear equation systems of the form Ax=b using direct or iterative solution algorithms.
Can derive solver equations to compute unknowns of interpolation or statistical regression functions used to model a data set.
Can numerically compute derivatives and integrals and solve first-order differential systems numerically.
Can predict the convergence, stability, or order of error of a method.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Basic topics, numbers, base concept, scientific notation, programming tools
Hafta 2: Modeling, computers and error analysis (truncation, rounding, Taylor series)
Hafta 3: Numerical solutions of nonlinear equations, f(x)=0
Hafta 4: Numerical approaches for solving algebraic equation systems A*x=b
Hafta 5: Numerical approaches for solving algebraic equation systems A*x=b
Hafta 13: Numerical solution of initial value problems
Hafta 14: Advanced topics: finite differences and boundary value problems, computational approaches to eigenvalue and eigenvector problems of symmetric matrices
resources: Steven C. Chapra, Raymond P. Canale, Numerical Methods for Engineers, 7th Edition, McGraw-Hill.
Timothy Sauer, Numerical Analysis, 7th Edition, Pearson.
Jaan Kiusalaas, Numerical Methods in Engineering with Python 3, Cambridge Unv. Press. (For sample codes)
http://nm.mathforcollege.com/NumericalMethodsTextbookUnabridged/ (onlinesource, offline content)

courses.name: Physics 1
courses.purpose: This course is the first of a sequence of two calculus-based introductory physics courses. It aims to introduce students to the fundamental laws of mechanics, strengthen their analytical thinking and problem-solving skills, and support the development of experimental skills through laboratory work.
courses.description: Vector algebra; kinematics in one, two, and three dimensions; dynamics and Newton's laws; work-energy principle; conservation of energy; linear momentum and its conservation; rotational kinematics; rotational dynamics; angular momentum and its conservation.
learning_outcomes: Understand and quantitatively describe the motion of objects using vector kinematics.
Solve dynamics problems by applying Newton's laws of motion.
Explain real-life phenomena using conservation laws of energy and linear momentum.
Gain skills in analytical thinking and the use of mathematical tools for problem solving and describing physical systems.
Develop skills in measurement, data collection and analysis, and presentation of experimental results through laboratory activities.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Science, Units, and Significant Figures
Hafta 2: Kinematics: Vectors, Kinematic Definitions, Motion in 1, 2, and 3 Dimensions
Hafta 3: Kinematics: Motion with Constant Acceleration, Free Fall
Hafta 4: Kinematics: Projectile Motion, Relative Velocity
Hafta 5: Dynamics: Newton's Laws of Motion
Hafta 6: Dynamics Applications: Friction, Uniform Circular Motion
Hafta 7: Dynamics Applications: Friction, Uniform Circular Motion / Continuation
Hafta 11: Linear Momentum and Its Conservation
Hafta 12: Linear Momentum and Its Conservation / Continuation
Hafta 13: Rotational Motion: Rotational Kinematics and Dynamics
resources: Physics for Scientists and Engineers with Modern Physics, Douglas C. Giancoli, Pearson, 4th Edition
Physics for Scientists and Engineers with Modern Physics, Serway, Jewett, Cengage Learning, 10th Edition

courses.name: Physics 2
courses.purpose: This course is the second of a sequence of two calculus-based introductory physics courses. Its purpose is to introduce students to the fundamental physical laws and principles of electromagnetism and to develop analytical thinking and problem-solving skills. Through laboratory work, it also aims to improve students’ experimental techniques.
courses.description: Electric charge and electric field, Gauss’s law, electric potential energy and electric potential, capacitors, Ohm’s law and resistance, DC circuits, magnetism and magnetic force, magnetic field and its sources, Ampere’s law, Biot-Savart law, electromagnetic induction, and Faraday’s law.
learning_outcomes: Understand the principles and applications of electromagnetism.
Solve electromagnetism-related problems using physical laws and principles.
Analyze and calculate electric potential, capacitance, and electric current in different circuit configurations.
Gain skills in analytical thinking and in using mathematical tools such as algebra and calculus for problem solving and describing physical systems.
Develop skills in measurement techniques, data collection, data analysis, and presentation through laboratory activities.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Electric Charge and Electric Field
Hafta 2: Electric Charge and Electric Field / continuation
Hafta 6: Capacitance, Dielectrics, Electric Energy Storage
Hafta 14: Electromagnetic Induction and Faraday’s Law
resources: Physics for Scientists and Engineers with Modern Physics, Douglas C. Giancoli, Pearson, 4th Edition
Physics for Scientists and Engineers with Modern Physics, Serway, Jewett, Cengage Learning, 10th Edition

courses.name: İstinyelilik Manifestosu 2
courses.purpose: İstinyelilik Manifestosu 2 dersi, öğrencilerin iş dünyasının hızla değişen ekonomik, sosyal, kültürel, etik ve yasal koşullarına uyum sağlamalarına, kariyerlerini şekillendirmelerine ve yönetmelerine yardımcı olmayı amaçlar. Ayrıca liderlik özellikleri ve takım ruhu kazandırarak öğrencilerin rekabetçi, bilgiye değer veren, evrensel etik değerlerle yaşayan, çalışkan, topluma karşı sorumlu ve sporcu özellikler geliştirmelerine katkı sağlar.

Yetenek ve yetkinlik kavramlarının kariyer gelişimindeki önemini kavrayabilir.

Hafta 13: Sektörel Firma Dersleri seminer etkinliklerine katılım
Hafta 14: Sektörel Firma Dersleri seminer etkinliklerine katılım

courses.name: Programming with C++
courses.purpose: This course provides an overview of programming concepts, computer program design, and programming with the C++ language. It aims to enable students to write programs in C++ and to build a foundation for the stages of software development, including program analysis, design, implementation, and testing.
courses.description: The course covers basic programming concepts, simple data types, constants, variables, identifiers, arithmetic operators, relational and logical operators, conditional structures, loops, functions, recursive functions, arrays, array sorting, searching in arrays, pointers, pass-by-reference, pass-by-value, and function pointers.
learning_outcomes: Defines basic programming concepts
Uses control structures in the C++ programming language
Designs functions in the C++ programming language
Uses arrays in the C++ programming language
Uses pointers in the C++ programming language
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to computer system concepts, computer components, operating system, file system, programming languages, compilers
Hafta 2: Simple data types, constants, variables, identifiers, arithmetic operators
Hafta 3: Conditional structures, relational and logical operators
Hafta 9: Character arrays, character array functions
Hafta 14: Passing by reference using pointers, function pointers
resources: C++ How to Program, 10/E, Paul Deitel, Harvey Deitel, ISBN-13: 9780134448237, Pearson.
https://cplusplus.com/doc/tutorial/

courses.name: Object Oriented Programming
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students object-oriented programming concepts so that they can make the software development process more effective and organized, and design software systems by applying OOP principles.
courses.description: Classes and objects, class member variables and functions, access specifiers and the concept of scope, function and operator overloading, data abstraction, inheritance and polymorphism, creation of abstract class definitions, class and function templates, introduction to design patterns.
learning_outcomes: Analyze and explain class definitions and standard procedures for object-oriented C++ approaches, including encapsulation (member data/functions).
Analyze programming code related to operator/function overloading in object-oriented approaches.
Analyze the inheritance hierarchy in a programming code.
Analyze the implementation details of polymorphism for object-oriented problems.
Analyze programming code related to the principles of class or function templates in object-oriented approaches.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming: introduction to classes and objects and arrays, class definitions, objects, data members, member functions, constructors, separation of interface.
Hafta 2: Classes: review of functions (scope concept, pointers, pointers and references, storage and lifetime of variables). Review of pointers, resolution operator (::).
Hafta 3: Classes: scope concept in classes, use of constructors and destructors. friend functions and friend classes, this pointer, class variables and object (instance) variables, case problems.
Hafta 4: Operator Overloading; string class: use of overloaded operators, overloading binary and unary operators, dynamic memory management.
Hafta 5: Operator Overloading; use of arrays and vectors from standard class templates, exception handling, examples of class applications of overloading with vectors and arrays.
Hafta 6: Object-Oriented Programming: inheritance concept, derived classes, inheritance hierarchy, constructors and destructors in derived classes.
Hafta 7: Object-Oriented Programming: software engineering with inheritance, examples.
Hafta 9: Object-Oriented Programming: polymorphism, abstract classes and virtual functions, virtual destructors
Hafta 11: Standard Library Containers and Iterators: containers, iterators, standard library algorithms
Hafta 12: Introduction to Special Templates: class templates, overloading of function templates
Hafta 13: Template-based Special Data Structures: self-referential structures.

Hafta 14: Other Topics in Object-Oriented Programming: variable class members, multiple inheritance, multiple inheritance and virtual base classes. Structural Design Patterns in C++. Example: decorator pattern.
resources: C++ How to Program, 10/E, Paul Deitel, Harvey Deitel, ISBN-13: 9780134448237, Pearson.
Bjarne Stroustrup, Programming: Principles and Practice Using C++, 2nd Edition, Pearson.

https://cplusplus.com/

courses.name: Computing Systems
courses.purpose: Analyze and explain the complex operation of computing systems, including interactions between hardware and software components. Understand the components and architecture of computer systems from basic building blocks such as processors, memory, and storage devices to more complex topics such as input/output systems, networks, and operating systems. Use critical thinking skills to analyze and solve problems related to computing systems, considering factors such as performance, efficiency, and compatibility. Design and optimize computing systems according to specific requirements and performance goals. Apply the fundamentals of logic design and integrated circuits to understand the underlying mechanisms of computer systems.
courses.description: This course provides a comprehensive exploration of the fundamental principles and concepts underlying modern computer systems. It investigates the complex operation of computing systems, including hardware, software, and their interactions. Students will gain a deep understanding of the components and architecture of computer systems, starting from basic building blocks such as processors, memory, and storage devices and extending to complex topics such as input/output systems, networks, and operating systems.
learning_outcomes: Evaluate the cache performance of a system given memory/cache characteristics and a specific address trace.
Identify various software vulnerabilities and how they can be exploited.
Manually simulate address translation as a way to understand hardware and software components that work similarly.
Experiment to determine efficient storage allocation strategies, especially stack memory allocation.
Organize code and use compiler settings to achieve improved performance on specific processor architectures.
resources: Randal E. Bryant and David R. O'Hallaron, Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective, Third Edition, Pearson, 2016
William Stallings. Computer Organization and Architecture, 11th Edition, Pearson; ISBN-13: 978-1-292-42010-3
David A. Patterson, John L. Hennessy. Computer Organization and Design, 6th Edition, Pearson; ISBN-13: 978-012820109
Computer Networking: a Top-Down Approach (8th ed.) J.F. Kurose, K.W. Ross, Pearson, 2020

courses.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.purpose: This course covers various topics including fundamental data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs, as well as more complex data structures such as hash tables and heaps. Students also gain knowledge of algorithm analysis and design, including sorting and searching algorithms. In addition, students learn algorithmic problem-solving techniques, algorithmic complexity, and Big-O notation, and complete programming assignments and projects that apply the concepts covered in lectures and readings.
courses.description: This course covers various topics including fundamental data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs, as well as more complex data structures such as hash tables and heaps. Students also gain knowledge of algorithm analysis and design, including sorting and searching algorithms. In addition, students learn algorithmic problem-solving techniques, algorithmic complexity, and Big-O notation, and complete programming assignments and projects that apply the concepts covered in lectures and readings.
learning_outcomes: Understand and apply the fundamental concepts of data structures and algorithms.
Understand and apply algorithm analysis and design techniques, including sorting and searching algorithms.
Analyze algorithmic problems and implement appropriate solutions using the concepts learned throughout the course.
Understand algorithmic complexity and Big-O notation, and apply them to analyze and compare different algorithms.
Develop efficient and optimized algorithms to solve programming problems.
resources: Ellis Horowitz, Sartaj Sahni and Dinesh Mehta, Fundamentals of Data Structure in C++, W.H.Freeman
Lecture notes

courses.name: Software Security
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of security principles, common vulnerabilities, and best practices in software development. It introduces the foundations of the secure development life cycle and extends to advanced topics such as memory injection attacks and web security, enabling students to identify, assess, and mitigate security risks in software systems.
courses.description: The course begins with an introduction to security principles and continues with the Secure Development Life Cycle for integrating security into the software development process. Students learn about various attack types, including memory, command, and script injection attacks. The course covers threat modeling and mitigation strategies, focusing on early detection and handling of security risks. Privacy-related topics include authentication, access control, and encryption. The course also emphasizes security testing and web security, helping students evaluate and improve the security of web applications.
learning_outcomes: Understand fundamental security principles and apply them to software systems.
Identify and mitigate common security vulnerabilities effectively.
Integrate security practices into the software development life cycle.
Adapt privacy measures and access controls to protect sensitive data.
Perform comprehensive security testing and assessments using standard techniques.
resources: Payer, M. (2019). Software Security: Principles, Policies, and Protection.
Dowd, M., McDonald, J., & Schuh, J. (2006). The art of software security assessment: Identifying and preventing software vulnerabilities. Pearson Education.
Mead, N. R., Allen, J. H., Barnum, S., Ellison, R. J., & McGraw, G. R. (2004). Software security engineering: a guide for project managers. Addison-Wesley Professional.
Helfrich, J. N. (2018). Security for Software Engineers. CRC Press.
Ransome, J., & Misra, A. (2018). Core software security: Security at the source.
Class Notes

courses.name: Software Systems Analysis and Design
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the tools, techniques, and knowledge base involved in each stage of the project management life cycle, including initiation, planning, execution, control, and closure. Students will gain insight into how project management is applied in different types of organizations and how it is an integral part of corporate strategy. The course also reviews software project management and explores the latest approaches, methodologies, and standards in software development.
courses.description: Software and Software Engineering, Software Process, Agile methods, analysis and design models, Software Engineering Practices, Web Engineering, component-based and service-oriented software development.
learning_outcomes: Define computer engineering concepts.
Requirements Engineering, Analysis Modeling
Design Engineering and Architecture and Component-Level Design
Agile methods and web application engineering
SOA and Software Quality
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction (software engineering disciplines, software products, software process, software engineering methods)
Hafta 2: System Development Life Cycle and Software Process (analysis, design, development, implementation and maintenance) and system documentation
Hafta 3: Software process models (classical models, iterative models, prototyping models, spiral models, agile models, scrum and XP)
Hafta 4: Prototyping and agile process models

Hafta 5: Introduction to object-oriented development; basic features of object-oriented systems; object-oriented system analysis and design (OOSAD); introduction to Unified Modeling Language (UML), structural and behavioral diagrams

Hafta 6: Requirements analysis and modeling: system requirements specification and analysis (define business processes, software requirements gathering, software requirements specification, functional and non-functional requirements), requirements models and analysis
Hafta 7: Design concepts and design models
Hafta 10: Architectural design, database and user interface design
Hafta 11: Implementation and maintenance models (introduction, system implementation, software testing, deployment, system documentation, training and maintenance of information systems)
Hafta 12: Analysis modeling for web applications and design modeling for web applications
resources: Eric J. Braude and Michael E. Bernstein, Software Engineering: Modern Approaches 2ed, John Wiley & Sons, 2011.
Lonnie D. Bentley and Jeffrey L. Whitten, Systems Analysis & Design for the Global Enterprise 7ed, McGraw Hill, 2007.
Prepared slides

courses.name: Programming Languages
courses.purpose: To provide an overview of the fundamental paradigms used in the development of modern programming languages. The course also aims to help students understand how source programs relate to their execution behavior and to introduce the formal theory behind programming language design.
courses.description: This course provides a comprehensive study of the fundamental concepts underlying programming languages and their execution models. Topics covered include values, variables, bindings, type systems, control structures, exceptions, concurrency, and modularity. Students learn how to choose and adapt different programming languages and compare their features as well as the cost-benefit tradeoffs of different implementations.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the fundamental issues and architectural interactions in programming language design
Compare the role of data types and implementation details in language design
Evaluate encapsulation and inheritance mechanisms in the context of object-oriented language design
Explain the implementation of program flow control mechanisms in language design
Design and analyze subprogram control mechanisms in programming languages
weekly_topics: Hafta 5: Introduction to Basic Data Types
Hafta 10: Vectors, Arrays, Records, Lists and Sets

courses.name: Software Project Management
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the tools, techniques, and body of knowledge involved in each stage of the project management life cycle, including initiation, planning, execution, control, and closure. Students gain insight into how project management is applied in different types of organizations and how it forms an integral part of corporate strategy. The course also examines software project management and explores the latest approaches, methodologies, and standards in software development.
courses.description: Understanding the tools, techniques, and body of knowledge involved in each stage of the software project management life cycle, including project initiation, planning, execution, control, and closure. Applying project management principles and practices to various types of organizations while recognizing the role of project management in achieving corporate strategy. Evaluating initial development costs and schedules for software projects by considering factors such as scope, resources, and constraints. Defining and planning test and prototyping activities to ensure the quality and functionality of software deliverables. Identifying and applying effective risk management approaches to recognize, analyze, and mitigate potential risks throughout the software project life cycle.
learning_outcomes: Understand what project management is, including project initiation, planning, execution, control, and closure.
Understand the tools, techniques, and body of knowledge involved in each stage of a project and the role of project management in achieving corporate strategy.
Evaluate initial development costs and schedules for software projects by considering factors such as scope, resources, and constraints.
Define and plan test and prototyping activities to ensure the quality and functionality of software deliverables.
Identify potential risks throughout the software project life cycle and determine effective risk management approaches to analyze and mitigate them.

Hafta 2: Project management, planning, execution, and control structures.
Hafta 3: Project definition and project components.
Hafta 4: Project planning and project progress.

Hafta 7: Calculating project cost and project cost components.
Hafta 8: Calculating the final cost of a software project.
Hafta 9: Definition of testing and prototyping activities; software quality and functionality.
Hafta 10: Testing under any disaster scenario.
Hafta 11: Potential risks in the software project life cycle.
Hafta 12: Effective risk management approaches and how to mitigate them.

courses.name: Software Architecture
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the fundamental principles and practices of software architecture within the software development life cycle. It focuses on developing students' ability to model and document software architectures using standard techniques such as UML, Architecture Description Languages (ADL), and the 4+1 architectural model. The course also emphasizes analyzing different architectural approaches and selecting appropriate ones, while considering quality attributes such as scalability, performance, and sustainability.
courses.description: The course covers the meaning and history of software architecture, project management, metrics, project planning, analysis concepts, analysis modeling, risk management in software projects, agile architecture, documenting software architectures with UML, programming paradigms, SOLID design principles, independence in software architecture, service-oriented architecture, serverless architecture, cloud-native applications, security, and DevOps.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental concepts of software architecture, architectural activities, the software development life cycle, and the role of architecture in software systems.
Model and document software architectures using Architecture Description Languages (ADL), UML, and the 4+1 architectural view model.
Analyze and compare different architectural styles and design patterns, including layered, client-server, component-based, and service-oriented architectures.
Design modern software systems using RESTful APIs, microservices, and distributed/cloud-based architectures supported by container technologies.
Integrate DevOps practices into the architectural design process and consider non-functional requirements such as scalability, performance, and sustainability.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Software Architecture (Definition and History of Software Architecture)
Hafta 2: Software Architecture Activities and the Software Development Life Cycle
Hafta 5: General Review of Architectural Styles
Hafta 8: Layered Architecture and Client-Server Architecture
Hafta 13: Distributed System Architecture (Cloud-Based Architecture, Docker and Kubernetes Fundamentals)
resources: L. Bass, P. Clements, R. Kazman. Software Architecture in Practice, Third Edition (or Fourth Edition). Addison-Wesley, 2013.
Robert Martin. Clean Architecture: A Craftsman`s Guide to Software Structure and Design, 1st Edition. Addison-Wesley, 2018.

courses.name: Web Programming
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide proficiency in fundamental web programming technologies including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and server-side scripting languages. It also aims to develop the ability to design and build interactive, dynamic web applications, integrate databases, and apply best practices in web development.
courses.description: The course covers web fundamentals, programming languages for the web, HTML basics, the working environment, PHP, using HTML with PHP, forms, sessions, cookies, CSS and templates, introduction to databases, database manipulation in PHP, JavaScript basics, browser and form programming with JavaScript, manipulating windows and frames, using dates, timers, string processing and regular expressions, DHTML, AJAX fundamentals, and security pitfalls with basic solutions.

Produce functional, flexible, and versatile websites.
Gain the ability to find, evaluate, and critically assess current and emerging technologies for evolving websites.
Have a good working knowledge of HTML5 and CSS.
Gain experience in creating various small website projects.
Gain awareness of the process of creating a website and the various roles needed in this process.

courses.name: Capstone Project 1
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to enable students to define a problem within a selected topic and carry out a thesis-style study individually or in a group through solution-oriented work.
courses.description: This course provides information on various topics related to the execution of engineering projects. The first six weeks cover topics that are generally relevant to the engineering departments of the faculty, and all engineering program students take a common exam on these topics. Students are also expected to reflect these topics in their reports and presentations. In addition, students are guided to form interdisciplinary teams and select a project from those proposed by faculty members. courses.name: Software Verification and Validation
courses.purpose: This course provides an in-depth study of various testing techniques and their applicability in ensuring software reliability. Students gain a comprehensive understanding of software verification and validation, including functional (black-box) testing, structural (white-box) testing, integration testing, mutation testing, model-based testing, and test case generation. The course also covers software reliability modeling and its connection to testing, while emphasizing the importance of reliability considerations. In addition, students are introduced to the testing process, testing tools, and automation techniques.
courses.description: Software is widely used in different systems such as transportation systems, medical services, embedded applications, home appliances, and mobile phones. Quality control in software testing is a systematic set of processes used to ensure the quality of software products or services. The main purpose of the quality control process is to ensure that the software product meets the actual requirements and contains minimal errors. Software testing refers to a process that improves the quality of software systems and is also one of the important stages in the software development process. In this course, students learn how to test software effectively. Programmers learn practical ways to design high-quality tests throughout all stages of software development. Students learn the theory behind criterion-based test design and apply this theory in practice. Topics include test design, test automation, test coverage criteria, and tools.
learning_outcomes: Students will learn standard effective, practical software testing techniques.
Students will design and implement high-quality tests for unit, integration, and regression testing.
Students will learn test automation techniques and become familiar with testing tools.
Students will learn mutation testing techniques and tools.

courses.name: Cryptography and Network Security
courses.purpose: This course covers various aspects of security threats and the measures taken to protect networks and information from attackers. It introduces students to attack types, security services, and security mechanisms, and includes several traditional and conventional encryption algorithms that can be applied in real-time scenarios.

courses.name: Data Mining

Special emphasis is given to machine learning methods, and related technologies such as data warehousing and online analytical processing (OLAP) are also discussed.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts, techniques, and tools used to extract valuable information from large datasets through topics such as data preprocessing, exploratory data analysis, predictive modeling, clustering, text mining, and project work.

courses.name: Natural Language Processing
courses.purpose: This course examines the algorithms and computational techniques that enable computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language. It integrates computational linguistics, machine learning, and speech processing to cover the full NLP pipeline, with emphasis on choosing appropriate methods based on task requirements, computational efficiency, and resource constraints.

It focuses on the use of the Java programming language in real-time systems and covers topics such as reliability, communication, scheduling, and real-time control.

courses.name: Distributed Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with knowledge about the architecture of distributed systems, middleware, system-level support, and the various aspects encountered in the design of distributed algorithms. It offers an in-depth study of design principles, challenges, and emerging paradigms for building and managing distributed computing systems. Students will also gain the ability to design and implement popular distributed system paradigms such as the Google File System and MapReduce through practical exercises and projects.
courses.description: Distributed systems are logically related collections of data distributed across different processing nodes of a computer network. In such systems, independent computers appear to users as a single unified computer. Hardware and software components in a distributed system communicate and coordinate their actions only through message passing. The course covers process distribution and communication, data distribution, scheduling, concurrency, resource sharing, synchronization, naming, abstraction and modularity, fault management, protection against accidental and malicious damage, distributed programming models, distributed file systems, virtualization, and the use of instrumentation, monitoring, and debugging tools. It also includes selected topics such as client/server challenges, partial failures, multiple address spaces, lack of a global clock, communication delays, heterogeneity, system administration, binding and naming, RPC and middleware, naming and directory services, distributed transaction processing, thin clients, data replication, cryptographic security, mobile code, and an introduction to Java RMI.
learning_outcomes: Understand design principles and distributed system architectures in distributed systems.
Explain the design, development, and applications of distributed systems.
Apply fundamental computer science methods and algorithms in the development of distributed systems and distributed system applications.
Discuss the design and testing of a large software system and communicate that design to others.
Analyze fault tolerance and recovery processes in distributed systems and the related algorithms.

courses.name: Insider Quality Assurance and Testing

In addition, through practical applications based on real-world scenarios, they will develop skills such as debugging, automation, and test planning, enabling them to contribute to the successful delivery of software projects.

courses.name: Agile and Software Testing Fundamentals
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students current and effective methods in software development processes and to help them understand how these methods are applied and what advantages they provide. It introduces the fundamental principles of Agile methodology, then covers the Scrum framework in detail, followed by the basic principles of software testing based on ISTQB Foundation Level, and finally Agile Testing practices.

It also covers Scrum roles, artifacts, and ceremonies; an introduction to software testing, test levels and techniques; test management, planning, monitoring and control, risk-based testing, and test tools; Agile testing principles and techniques; and practical work such as Scrum simulations, test case writing, and test automation applications.

courses.name: QNB Essentials of Strategy: From Concept to Execution!
courses.purpose: After completing this course, students are expected to be able to explain the fundamental components of strategy, identify and improve the fit between a company’s strategy and organizational structure in a competitive environment, analyze business challenges and propose solution-oriented approaches using core strategic concepts, interpret and evaluate business processes through engineering thinking, systems analysis, and value creation frameworks, assess the impact of digital transformation on organizational design, customer experience, and strategic decision-making, demonstrate leadership and collaboration skills in team-based activities by solving complex real-world problems through role-playing games and simulations, communicate strategic ideas effectively across different stakeholder groups using storytelling, structured presentations, and persuasive techniques, and reflect on personal and professional development by setting growth goals and understanding how to influence and lead in complex organizational settings even without formal authority.
courses.description: The course is structured around the fundamental components of strategy and aims to equip students with key concepts and tools they are likely to encounter in professional life. Core themes include strategic management, value creation, process design, digital transformation, organizational dynamics, and customer-oriented innovation, each approached from a strategic perspective to bridge theory and real-world application. The topics integrate engineering perspectives with business problem solving, digital process development, and leadership competencies to drive change. Students engage in case studies, role-playing games, and collaborative exercises designed to simulate real business challenges and promote experiential learning. The course emphasizes practical thinking, analytical rigor, and cross-functional collaboration. It also develops students’ ability to navigate complex organizational environments, influence without formal authority, and communicate ideas effectively to diverse stakeholders. With a focus on reflective thinking and active participation, the course prepares students to confidently apply strategic frameworks and become emerging professionals ready to lead and adapt in rapidly changing industries.
learning_outcomes: Analyze business problems from an engineering perspective and develop solutions using digital tools.
Create value through customer-oriented thinking and user experience design.
Take an active role in business life with change management, leadership, and communication skills.
Develop strategic thinking and problem-solving competencies through real case studies and group projects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, What is the Goal?
Hafta 2: Organizational Structures, Analysis of Resources and Capabilities
Hafta 3: Understanding a Business Process from an Engineering Perspective
Hafta 4: Creating Value with Customer Journey Mapping and User Experience Design
Hafta 7: Change Management and Human Dynamics in Organizational Change
Hafta 10: Collaboration and Leadership: Excelling as an Effective Participant
Hafta 11: Fundamentals of Financial Literacy in Business Environments

courses.name: Generative AI
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a theoretical understanding of the fundamental components of generative AI systems and to develop comprehensive knowledge of contemporary approaches such as large language models (LLMs), transformer architectures, prompt engineering, vector databases, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). It also aims to enable students to critically analyze current literature on agentic AI concepts, ethical challenges, and the responsible deployment of generative AI systems.
courses.description: The course begins with the fundamentals of machine learning and deep learning, then covers the classification of generative models, transformer architecture, the structure of large language models (LLMs), prompt engineering techniques, and core concepts such as embeddings and vector databases. It then theoretically examines the logic, components, and frameworks of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems. Next, it discusses the conceptual structure of agentic AI systems, including multi-agent coordination and governance strategies. Finally, it addresses critical topics such as security, ethics, and evaluation metrics, and analyzes current academic and technological trends in generative AI.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental components of generative AI systems, the basic principles of large language models (LLMs), and transformer architecture.
Analyze and apply the working mechanisms of large language models, including training strategies such as pretraining, fine-tuning, and RLHF, as well as prompt engineering techniques.
Interpret and compare retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems in terms of their structure and components; explain the role of embeddings and vector databases in information retrieval.

Evaluate agentic AI systems in terms of coordination and decision-making processes; examine the ethical, security, and regulatory dimensions of generative AI and follow current developments in the field.

Hafta 2: Introduction to Generative AI and Model Categories
Hafta 3: Large Language Model Architectures and Learning Approaches

courses.name: Data Mining for Cybersecurity
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the ability to understand threats encountered in cybersecurity and to effectively use data mining, machine learning, and analytical methods to combat them. It also aims to develop competencies in analyzing attack types from a data-driven perspective, detecting vulnerabilities and anomalies, and contributing to intelligence systems with data-supported insights.
courses.description: This course begins with an introduction to the fundamental principles of cybersecurity, threat types, and data sources, then continues with cryptology, risk analysis, network vulnerabilities, and defense techniques. In the second part of the course, students study attack detection, anomaly analysis, time-series-based threat prediction, and threat intelligence applications with large language models (LLMs) using machine learning and data mining methods. Students develop projects on real cybersecurity datasets and present a comprehensive data-driven security solution at the end of the term.
learning_outcomes: Analyze cybersecurity threats and explain basic defense principles.
Apply data mining and machine learning techniques to security data.
Develop attack detection models using real datasets.
Produce solutions while considering ethics, privacy, and security responsibilities.

courses.name: History of Science and Ethics
courses.purpose: This course provides a general overview of the historical development of science from antiquity to the modern era, and also introduces research ethics, engineering responsibility, and current issues in science and technology.
courses.description: What is science? Ancient scientific traditions and the beginnings of the scientific method; the Scientific Revolution; Copernicus, Galileo, Newton, and the birth of modern science; the Enlightenment and industrial foundations; mechanics, thermodynamics, chemistry, electricity, and the rise of engineering; 19th century developments; evolution, classical electromagnetism, and atomic theory; 20th century revolutions; relativity, quantum mechanics, and molecular biology; Big Science; the Manhattan Project, the space race, CERN, and science and government; philosophy of science; Popper, Kuhn, Lakatos, models, and scientific reasoning; objectivity, bias, and reproducibility; values in science and the limits of objectivity; research ethics and integrity; plagiarism, fabrication, authorship, and open science; engineering ethics; risk, safety, responsibility, and major engineering disasters; AI and technology ethics; algorithmic bias, data ethics, and accountability; sustainability and environmental ethics; climate science, environmental justice, and sustainable engineering; science and society; global inequalities, public trust in science, and colonial science; course synthesis and integrative discussion.
learning_outcomes: Understand the major turning points in the history of science.
Analyze the relationship between science, technology, and society.
Recognize ethical issues in scientific research and engineering practice.
Apply ethical frameworks to case studies and real-world scenarios.
Develop informed perspectives on contemporary technological challenges.
weekly_topics: Hafta 14: Course Synthesis and Integrative Discussion
resources: David C. Lindberg, The Beginnings of Western Science
Thomas Kuhn, The Structure of Scientific Revolutions
David B. Resnik, The Ethics of Science: An Introduction
Harris, Pritchard & Rabins, Engineering Ethics
Additional weekly readings (articles/chapters) provided by instructor

courses.name: Lean Manufacturing and Management Principles
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of Lean Thinking and the Toyota Production System (TPS). It focuses on developing the ability to identify and eliminate waste, improve process efficiency, and enhance quality across various sectors. Students will learn to apply Lean principles, tools, and techniques such as 5S, Kaizen, Kanban, and Value Stream Mapping to design and implement continuous improvement strategies. The course also emphasizes performance measurement using Lean metrics and KPIs, preparing participants to manage change effectively and foster a culture of operational excellence.
courses.description: The course covers the fundamentals of Lean philosophy, including the five core principles: Value, Value Stream, Flow, Pull, and Perfection. Topics include types of waste and their impact on business performance, Value Stream Mapping, visual management tools, and the 5S methodology. Students will examine Kaizen practices, Lean tools such as Kanban, Poka-Yoke, Jidoka, and the PDCA cycle, as well as performance measurement techniques and KPI dashboard design. Advanced modules address Lean supply chain management, Theory of Constraints, Lean quality management with TQM and Six Sigma integration, and Lean transformation and change management strategies. Real-world applications and case studies from manufacturing, healthcare, and service sectors, including examples from Amazon, Pixar, and Virginia Mason Healthcare, reinforce practical understanding.
learning_outcomes: Understand and explain Lean Thinking and Toyota Production System (TPS) principles, including core concepts such as value, flow, and waste elimination.
Apply Lean tools and techniques such as 5S, Kaizen, Kanban, and Value Stream Mapping to analyze processes and implement continuous improvement strategies.
Evaluate performance using Lean metrics and KPIs, and design practical solutions for efficiency improvement, quality enhancement, and waste reduction across different sectors.

Hafta 2: Lean Thinking and Types of Waste, Impact of waste on business performance, examples of waste analysis
Hafta 3: Value Stream Mapping, current state and future state maps, examples of VSM drawing
Hafta 4: Visual Management, visual management tools (Kanban, Andon), 5S methodology and implementation steps
Hafta 5: Kaizen (Continuous Improvement), Kaizen philosophy and implementation methods, Kaizen events (Kaizen Blitz), developing improvement suggestions
Hafta 6: Lean tools and techniques, Kanban, Poka-Yoke, Jidoka, PDCA cycle, Kanban board design
Hafta 7: Performance Measurement and KPIs, Lean metrics (Takt Time, Lead Time, PCE), KPI definition and monitoring, KPI dashboard design
Hafta 8: Lean Supply Chain Management, JIT, inventory management, demand-driven systems
Hafta 9: Theory of Constraints and Bottleneck Management, 5 focusing steps, bottleneck analysis and solution strategies
Hafta 10: Lean Quality Management, quality improvement techniques (TQM, Six Sigma integration), error prevention (Poka-Yoke), FMEA, error analysis and solution proposals

courses.name: Introduction to Sociology
courses.purpose: This course teaches students the basic elements of the discipline of sociology. By the end of the semester, students will have basic knowledge about the sources of social conflict and cooperation. They will develop critical and analytical thinking skills for examining society and human interactions.
courses.description: Sociology can be defined as the systematic study of human society and social life. This course helps students make sociological sense by exploring questions such as: How is human behavior shaped? What are the foundations of social conflict and cooperation? What social processes affect people's ways of thinking, feelings, thought structures, and behaviors? How do people shape the social pressures they encounter? Students will also learn basic sociological theories, including structural functionalism, social conflict, symbolic interactionism, and feminism.

Learn critical sociological thinking

courses.name: Sociology of Inequality

courses.name: Introduction to Business Functions
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to fundamental business concepts and practices, inform them about the business environment, business ethics and corporate social responsibility, entrepreneurship, and the global business context, and provide an understanding of management, organization, motivation, leadership, and marketing.
courses.description: This course is designed to introduce students to the business world and show how businesses operate in a changing business environment. The course provides students with information about the business environment, business ethics and corporate social responsibility, entrepreneurship, and the global business context, and lays a foundation in management, organization, motivation, leadership, and marketing.
learning_outcomes: Define basic business concepts and practices.
Explain the business environment, business ethics and corporate social responsibility, entrepreneurship, and the global business context.
Outline concepts and practices in business management and organization.
Analyze issues in management, organization, motivation, leadership, and marketing.
