courses.name: International Business Environment
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to environmental forces in international business, environmental forecasting, and environmental analysis; explain major issues in the international competitive, economic, and technological environments; familiarize students with the international cultural, demographic, and social environment; provide an understanding of ecological environmental issues; inform students about the international political and legal environment; and enable students to research, prepare, and present a project as part of a team.
courses.description: This course covers the main areas of the international business environment for future managers who will engage in and be affected by international ventures. It addresses innovations and developments in transportation, production, communication, and technology that are leading to a truly global economy, and the need to understand the subtle differences of doing business overseas and across borders.
learning_outcomes: Define basic business concepts and practices related to environmental forces, environmental forecasting, and environmental analysis in international trade.
Analyze the forces that create competition in the international business environment.
Explain the major issues in international economic and technological environments.
Evaluate the elements of the international cultural, demographic, and social environment.
Identify the major issues in international ecological, political, and legal environments.
Conduct research, prepare a project, and present it as a team member.

courses.name: Design Thinking
courses.purpose: This course introduces User-Centered Design and Design Thinking through some basic principles of interaction design for products and services. It focuses on human needs, preferences, and experiences rather than only technological factors, and it teaches an empathetic approach that helps students connect with users, ask design questions, and generate project ideas.
courses.description: The course provides theoretical, conceptual, and practical knowledge and skills that may be useful when designing products, services, interaction systems, and systems in general within the framework of Design Thinking and human-centered design.
learning_outcomes: Explain the principles and examples of empathic design and instinctive design.
Apply the basic methods of User-Centered Design to define personas and target audiences.
Create an innovative and original project within the framework of the three basic criteria of User-Centered Design: feasible technology, viable business plan, and empathic design.

courses.name: Gender and Media
courses.purpose: This course examines the role of media in constructing gender and its intersections with race, ethnicity, class, and sexuality. It also considers the importance of diversity in media industries and the role of new media technologies in challenging and/or reaffirming traditional gender structures.
courses.description: This course examines representations of race, class, gender, and sexual identity in the media. Students explore authorship, audiences, and the ways in which various media contents, including film, television, print journalism, and advertising, enable, facilitate, and challenge social structures in society. The course also investigates how gender and race affect media production and discusses gender through the impact of new media and digital media, drawing on contemporary media users. Students analyze sexist and racialized language and regulation as it is produced online in blogs, vlogs, avatars, and the construction of cyber identities. The course provides an introduction to feminist approaches to media studies, drawing on work in feminist film theory, journalism, cultural studies, gender and politics, and cyberfeminism.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes diversity among audiences, content, and media producers.
Identifies stereotypes of gender, race, class, and sexual identity in media representations.
Finds examples of framing, intersectionality, and symbolic annihilation in the media.
Analyzes texts in the context of cultural and social identities, considering how reality is socially and discursively constructed by the media.
Discusses media literacy in contemporary terms in light of 21st-century developments in online cultural production and new media.
Understands the basic theories and methods of examining media, power, and social identities.

courses.name: History of Economics
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to provide students with a basic understanding of the historical development of economic processes and institutions, changes in production, patterns of distribution and consumption, and the general development of production factors in the world, especially in Western Europe.
courses.description: The course begins with some concepts and definitions related to economics and economic history. It continues with the economic understandings of ancient civilizations, the Middle Ages, and the pre-capitalist world. The emergence of the modern economy at the dawn of the Industrial Revolution is analyzed. With the Industrial Revolution, modern economy and its many new institutions are introduced, and modern civilizations and the modern world economy up to the 21st century are covered.
learning_outcomes: Have a general understanding of the history of the world economy.
Learn the differences between leading and follower economies in world economic history.
Understand the reasons for the successes and failures of leading and follower nations in history.

courses.name: Economics of Technology & Innovation
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students’ analytical understanding of the economics of technology and innovation. It examines how innovation drives economic growth, reshapes competition, and transforms industries and institutions, while also helping students understand the roles of firms, financial systems, institutions, and public policy in supporting technological change in modern economies.
courses.description: This course examines innovation as a structural force that drives long-term economic growth and reshapes capitalist economies. It addresses the limits of capital accumulation and explains productivity dynamics through Solow and Schumpeterian frameworks. The course analyzes firm behavior, competition, institutions, and national innovation systems to understand how innovation emerges, spreads, and transforms economic structures. It also examines historical technological revolutions, catching-up processes, and structural transformation. The course concludes with discussions on inequality, finance, competition policy, and the sustainability of innovation-driven capitalism.
learning_outcomes: Explain the relationship between innovation, productivity dynamics, and long-term economic growth.
Analyze how firms compete through innovation in dynamic and uncertain markets.
Evaluate the role of institutions, financial systems, and public policy in shaping innovation systems.
Compare countries’ development paths in terms of technological capability, absorptive capacity, and structural transformation.
Apply key theoretical frameworks (Solow, Schumpeterian, and evolutionary approaches) to interpret technological change and its broader economic consequences, including inequality and sustainability.

courses.name: Freewill
courses.purpose: This course aims to explore, explain, and discuss various philosophical arguments concerning the concept of free will in historical and thematic terms.
courses.description: This course provides students with an introductory understanding of the fundamental philosophical questions related to the concept of free will. It begins with an introductory section focused on the conceptual framework of will, free will, and determinism. Then, following a historical sequence, it examines the approaches of ten thinkers from Ancient Greece to the twentieth century under four modules. Based on the discussions throughout the term, important philosophical issues closely connected to free will, such as individual responsibility, the problem of evil, the possibility of morality, philosophical freedom, and the foundations of law and justice, are discussed.

Students will gain knowledge of debates on some important philosophical questions throughout the history of philosophy.
Students will learn to reason creatively.
Students will learn to think critically about fundamental philosophical problems.
Students will develop a historical perspective on different areas of philosophy.

courses.name: History of Cosmology
courses.purpose: This course aims to present the history of cosmological theories chronologically and in broad outline, without requiring prior knowledge of physics, mathematics, or geometry. It also aims to help students understand how human thought, belief systems, and practical skills evolved through this process, and to encourage scientific thinking and curiosity about research.
courses.description: Starting from prehistoric civilizations, the course examines how humanity's understanding of astronomy and later cosmology developed, and how this accumulated knowledge was used to answer questions about the universe. It covers the roles of religion, practical applications such as calendars, and later scientific developments involving mathematics, geometry, and physics. Students also gain basic information about prehistoric and historical civilizations and become acquainted with scientists and thinkers.
learning_outcomes: Explains the emergence and development of knowledge related to cosmology in prehistoric and historical periods in broad terms.
States the main outlines of basic popular knowledge about the history of cosmology and current theories.

Hafta 4: Concepts of the universe in Ancient China

Hafta 6: Understanding of the universe among Turks before Islam
Hafta 7: Understanding of the universe among Turks after Islam
Hafta 9: Concepts of the universe in Ancient Greek civilization
Hafta 10: Ideas about the universe during the Hellenistic and Roman periods
Hafta 11: Concepts of the universe in the medieval Christian and Islamic world
Hafta 12: Concepts of the universe in the Renaissance and Enlightenment periods
Hafta 13: Theories about the universe in the 19th century
Hafta 14: Theories about the universe in the 20th century
Hafta 15: Theories about the universe in the 21st century

courses.name: Introduction to Political Sociology
courses.purpose: This course focuses on the network of relations among state, individual, and society within the general framework of the basic concepts and theoretical approaches of political sociology and in light of current debates. It aims to make sense of social and political formations taking place in the contemporary world by opening up the major theories of political sociology.
courses.description: The course covers elites, social classes, the state, political parties and processes, media, social movements and related organizations, and the structures shaping modern societies such as market, state, and civil society, as well as power, domination, legitimacy, the state, elites and their social networks, social class and inequality, protest, collective action, and social movements.
learning_outcomes: Has the ability to understand theories and theoretical perspectives in sociology and the social sciences.
Has the knowledge to use basic theoretical analyses and sociological concepts in their own work.
Has the knowledge and skills to interpret social, economic, and historical phenomena sociologically by reasoning within a social, economic, and historical framework.
Has the ability to follow and evaluate classical and contemporary studies and research in Turkish and English in the world in their own field.
Uses the theoretical framework and research methods appropriate for sociological research in a future project.
Relates research questions to the findings obtained as a result of the research and reports the results.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the political sociology course, beginning with two striking historically significant examples: Magna Carta and the Rosa Parks event.
Hafta 2: The pioneering position in political sociology of Thorstein Veblen (1857-1929), praised by the German social scientist Max Gustav Lange.
Hafta 3: Political sociology as an interdisciplinary subfield of sociology, shaped from the early 1930s in the context of developments such as communism, fascism, and the Second World War.
Hafta 4: Other relevant thinkers: Machiavelli, Tocqueville, M. Weber, K. Marx, C.W. Mills. Two prominent Turkish social scientists: Niyazi Berkes and Behice Boran.

Hafta 5: A comprehensive analysis of the theme of power, covering all relevant dimensions: definition, related analogies, types of power, related networks, institutionalized power in a society, visibility and power of women, and the ancient Turkish concept of "kut," which regarded governing as a divine gift.
Hafta 6: Institutions that especially demonstrate the visibility and implementation of power, such as the army, police, and prison administration.

Hafta 7: The international dimension of power, where a stronger state can somehow influence a weaker state; examples include the return of Turkic new states to the Russian Cyrillic alphabet after a long interval, and the historical lines of Japanese, Prussian, American, and Turkish military traditions.

courses.name: Introduction to Metaverse
courses.purpose: The main objective of the course is to develop within students an understanding of the core components of the metaverse and an awareness of its potential impacts on society. By the end of the class, students will possess developed ethical positions on many of the important metaverse topics.
courses.description: The course introduces fundamental elements that form the foundation of various conceptualizations of “The Metaverse”. Topics to be presented and discussed include shared spatialization, digital mediation of reality, socialization, and assigning value to digital objects. The course will devote significant time to discussions of ethics and the impacts that digitization will have on non-digital aspects of society. Students will be required to research and write several essays throughout the course and design a metaverse scenario as a final group project. Online class sessions will frequently take place inside of 2D and 3D “metaverse platforms”. Students will be expected to know how to use their keyboard and mouse/touchpad to navigate these spaces and to use their microphone effectively. This is not a course focused on cryptographic topics. Blockchains, cryptocurrencies, and NFTs will not be the focus of the course, although these subjects will be included in discussions of metaverse economics and concepts of ownership.

courses.name: Unhealthy Brilliants
courses.purpose: In this course, students will learn about famous people who have achieved success in the past and present, while also living with chronic diseases. The course aims to raise awareness about the effort, perseverance, and determination needed to overcome difficulties on the path to success.
courses.description: The course discusses the concepts of "brilliant" and "unhealthy" and examines well-known successful figures together with the chronic illnesses they have experienced. Topics include alcoholism/substance use disorder, hepatitis B/liver cirrhosis, sexually transmitted diseases, malignancies, obesity/anorexia, diabetes, depression, burnout syndrome, acne, congenital malformations, asthma, celiac disease, atheromatous disease, chronic kidney disease, autoimmune diseases, Alzheimer disease, and infectious diseases through examples of famous people.
learning_outcomes: Students will gain awareness of the effort, perseverance, and determination needed to overcome difficulties on the path to success through the lives of successful people with illnesses.
Congenital anomalies will be discussed through national swimmer Sümeyye Boyacı.
Celiac disease will be discussed through famous tennis player Novak Djokovic.
Hepatitis B/cirrhosis and the life of composer Beethoven will be discussed.
Malignancies (leukemia, lymphoma, etc.) and Tetsuya "Ted" Fujita's brain cancer will be discussed.
Sexually transmitted diseases and Michael C. Hall (HPV-related lymphoma) will be discussed.
Asthma and Nilüfer will be discussed.
Alcoholism/substance use disorder and Robert Downey Jr. will be discussed.
Burnout syndrome and Eric Yuan will be discussed.
Acne and Emma Stone will be discussed.
Abdelhalim Hafiz and schistosomiasis will be discussed.
Atheromatous disease and Avul Pakir Jainulabdeen Abdul Kalam (cardiac arrest) will be discussed.
Chronic kidney disease and Aries Merritt will be discussed.
Autoimmune diseases and Renoir (rheumatoid arthritis) will be discussed.
Alzheimer disease and Gabriel García Márquez (lymphoma and AD) will be discussed.

courses.description: In this course, daily and collective rhythms, possibilities, and qualities of social life shaped by social and ideological practices in crisis spaces where various cultural, economic, and political conflicts intersect in different geographies of the world will be discussed using sociology literature.
learning_outcomes: Develop the ability to discuss conceptual thinking practices through spatial and social structures.
Develop the ability to conduct research and literature review.
Develop the ability to write articles and cite references based on research.
Develop presentation skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction What is architecture as a discipline?
Hafta 2: Relationship between space and place
Hafta 10: Spatial agency and alternative practices

courses.name: Introduction to Marketing
courses.purpose: This course provides a comprehensive introduction to contemporary marketing practices. It aims to help students understand fundamental marketing concepts, apply up-to-date frameworks and techniques to analyze marketing problems, and analyze and develop a marketing plan.
courses.description: The course covers market planning, market research, consumer behavior, advertising and promotion, branding, marketing strategy, distribution, pricing, product management, global marketing, and sustainable marketing. Students will apply theories to real-life examples and cases throughout the course, in addition to an assignment focused on launching a new product.
learning_outcomes: Understand marketing as a process and function
Understand the tools used for market segmentation and planning
Understand the elements of the marketing mix and their roles in value creation and delivering value to consumers
Understand the importance of marketing in creating a sustainable competitive advantage
Understand and evaluate the opportunities and challenges organizations may face and how these shape marketing decision-making
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Market Research and Marketing Analytics
Hafta 5: Marketing Strategy: Segmentation, Targeting and Positioning
Hafta 6: Consumer and Business Markets: Consumer Behavior and B2B Marketing
Hafta 7: Product Management 1: Innovation and New Product Development
Hafta 8: Product Management 2: Product Strategy and Branding
Hafta 10: Personal Selling, Public Relations, Social Media and Direct/Database Marketing

courses.description: This course covers the communication process, verbal and nonverbal communication in interpersonal relationships, organizational communication, perception and persuasive communication, and media studies.
learning_outcomes: Understand the communication process.
Comprehend the role of verbal and nonverbal communication skills in effective communication.
Recognize the impact of perception in communication.
Understand communication processes occurring in different contexts.

Hafta 3: Verbal Communication - The Importance of Listening
Hafta 12: Media Literacy and Digital Communication

courses.name: Advanced Extended Reality Projects
courses.purpose: To learn virtual reality, augmented reality, and mixed reality technologies. To understand the application areas of virtual reality technologies. To develop learning and project ideas for project development processes.
courses.description: The history and development of virtual reality technologies and the technologies used for their application are examined with examples. The production processes of a virtual reality project are learned.
learning_outcomes: To learn the use of virtual reality technologies in different sectors
To be able to evaluate XR projects for fields such as education, art, medicine, and engineering
To learn the features that should be present in a successful virtual reality project

courses.name: Digital Media and Social Media Management
courses.purpose: The general purpose of this course is to examine and learn all stages, characteristics, dynamics, elements, and application methods of the transfer of the marketing concept to cyber environments as a result of developing technological and communication opportunities. Among the general aims of the course are to examine and learn digital marketing models, advertising and public relations practices developed in digital environments, new concepts and evolving marketing strategies, digital marketing campaigns, brand positioning in digital environments, e-commerce, and mobile marketing applications.
courses.description: This course covers search engine advertising, search engine optimization, the web concept, social media, and changing e-commerce trends in digital environments. It presents data and applications related to the digitalization of the 4P approach, which is defined as the marketing mix. In addition, digital brand and digital marketing trends are discussed in a world where social media has transformed from content into an e-commerce environment.
learning_outcomes: To examine concepts related to cyberspace and have knowledge of evaluating processes within their historical development
To learn the characteristics of digital marketing strategies and applications
To examine digital campaign applications and gain competence in actively applying them
To follow the developments in digital marketing dynamics, design future scenarios, and gain the ability to evaluate the digital world from a marketing perspective in the context of trade
To be able to use digital marketing tools in their basic aspects and analyze performance and campaign metrics by reading them
To become familiar in advance with the concept of S-Commerce, the near-future trend of trade
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition, elements, and development of digital marketing. Similar and different aspects, advantages and disadvantages of conventional and digital marketing
Hafta 2: Digital marketing advertising models – Banner, Search, Video & In-Stream, e-mail, etc.
Hafta 3: Consumer targeting models in digital marketing. APPLICATION: Creating a new campaign and determining a targeting method through the Google Ads panel
Hafta 4: Performance measurement parameters – Volume, Impression, Click, Conversion, Action. APPLICATION: Interpreting data from a real campaign
Hafta 5: Pricing and performance conversion metrics – CPM, CPC, CPA, etc. APPLICATION: Examining and interpreting performance metrics of a real campaign
Hafta 6: Supporting digital marketing applications (SMS, Mailing, WebPush, App Push)
Hafta 7: SEO – Search engine optimization: Definition of the concept, basic components
Hafta 9: (SERP) – Elements required to be listed in search engines (Title, Meta-Description, h1-h2-h3 headings, etc.)
Hafta 10: SEO analysis tools – Supporting applications (backlink, Domain Rating, etc.)

Hafta 11: Social media as a digital marketing channel. Influencer Marketing – Social Marketing and S-Commerce concepts. Performance measurement metrics such as interaction, likes, and shares (engagement)
Hafta 12: Facebook and Instagram Ads. Steps of creating a social media campaign. APPLICATION: Interpreting data from a real campaign
Hafta 13: Influencer marketing. S-Commerce concept and content management in social media (Content Marketing)
Hafta 14: Site analysis tools – Introduction to Google Analytics. APPLICATION: Interpreting site performance metrics of a real website

courses.name: Sign Language
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip university students with sign language skills to improve communication opportunities with hearing and speech-impaired people in society and to help students differentiate themselves in their future careers. It also introduces the basic skills of producing and understanding Turkish Sign Language (TİD), including manual alphabet, numbers, conversational strategies, and culturally appropriate behavior.
courses.description: The course introduces the production and comprehension of Turkish Sign Language (TİD) at a basic level. It covers manual alphabet, numbers, greetings, self-introduction, pronouns, question forms, daily vocabulary and dialogues, emotions, 5W1H questions, verbs, time concepts, money, months, days, seasons, weather, family, colors, clothing, opposites, fruits, vegetables, and sentence construction practice.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate comprehensive mastery of target vocabulary items and high-frequency words in Turkish Sign Language at A1-A2 level.
Show awareness of TİD grammar, vocabulary, and phonology/cheirology, including finger spelling practice.
Use familiar words, phrases, and sentences to present information to others in TİD.
Demonstrate receptive and productive speaking/signing skills in short dialogues and topic-based conversations.
Apply communication-facilitating and regulating behaviors such as attention-getting and turn-taking.
Participate in cultural activities and use technology to communicate and practice the language inside and outside the classroom.
Understand Deaf Turkish culture, TİD, and its history, and compare their own culture with Turkish Deaf culture.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Hearing differences and Deaf culture; alphabet
Hafta 2: Greeting dialogue; self-introduction in sign language; pronouns; question facial expressions; basic daily vocabulary and dialogues
Hafta 3: Positive and negative facial expressions; question examples; practice with learned words
Hafta 4: Emotions; example sentences with emotions
Hafta 8: Time concept; clock concept; money concept
Hafta 9: Practice on time, clock, and money concepts; question facial expressions
Hafta 10: Months, days, seasons, weather events; verbs (10 verbs); practice with verbs
Hafta 11: Family and genealogy; describe your own family
Hafta 12: Colors and clothes; clothing description
Hafta 13: Opposites; sentence practice with opposites
Hafta 14: Fruits, vegetables, other foods; sentence construction and sentence practice
resources: Lecturer's notes

courses.name: Tracking Nobel Prizes!
courses.purpose: This course aims to familiarize students with scientific studies not only in their own field but also in other fields such as Physics, Chemistry, and Psychology. By focusing on interesting studies especially in the field of history, it also aims to increase students' interest in scientific research. In addition, the course emphasizes some recent Nobel Prizes and the content of related works, encouraging students to generate ideas on these topics. Students are also encouraged to examine scientific research and think about its effects on the future of humanity.
courses.description: Definition and characteristics of science, a brief overview of scientific developments in the 19th and 20th centuries, science and research, steps of scientific research methods, the Nobel Prize, Nobel Prize laureates, and important Nobel Prizes in the field of health in recent history.
learning_outcomes: Defines the concepts of science and scientific research.
Defines the difference between hypothesis and theory.
Explains the scientific research process.

Hafta 3: Important Scientific Developments in the 20th Century

Hafta 14: Nobel prizes of the previous year 1

courses.name: Disaster and Emergency Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students from different disciplines to disaster management phases, crisis and risk management concepts, the authorities and responsibilities of stakeholders in disaster management, disaster laws and regulations, and community-based organizational structures. It also aims to explain practical applications such as risk, hazard and SWOT analysis.
courses.description: The course covers concepts related to disasters, types and characteristics of disasters, integrated disaster management, its basic principles and phases, disaster cycle, risk and crisis management, mitigation, preparedness, response and recovery phases. It also examines the economic dimensions of disasters, risk transfer methods such as disaster funds and insurance, SWOT/risk analysis, actors and their roles in disaster management, disaster management structures in Turkey, laws, regulations and institutions, post-1999 and post-2023 earthquake assessments, and examples of disaster management systems from Turkey and the world.
learning_outcomes: Understand the phases of disaster management and the concepts of crisis and risk management.
Recognize the actors in disaster management and their authorities and responsibilities.
Learn risk transfer methods.
Know the disaster management structure, laws and regulations in Turkey.
Understand the Incident Command System and its applications.
Identify risk, hazard and SWOT analysis applications.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Concepts and definitions related to disasters (hazard, risk, what is a disaster? Difference between hazard and risk)
Hafta 2: Types and characteristics of disasters; is every hazard a disaster? What is disaster management? Basic principles of disaster management

Hafta 3: What is Integrated Disaster Management (IDM)? Basic principles and phases; basic information about response, recovery, mitigation and preparedness phases; disaster cycle; risk and crisis management concepts
Hafta 4: Mitigation phase (economic dimensions of disasters, risk transfer methods: disaster funds and insurance, DASK example)

Hafta 5: Preparedness phase (early warning and rapid response, scenarios, emergency action plans); institutional preparedness studies, disaster plan preparation, role and importance of planning in disaster management, planning process, institutional planning examples
Hafta 6: Response phase (search and rescue, first aid, fire, security); drills, their purpose, types and outcomes
Hafta 7: Incident Command System, creation of institutional ICSs and criteria
Hafta 9: Recovery / reconstruction phase (temporary and permanent housing, solving infrastructure problems, implementation of mitigation measures, current practice examples)
Hafta 10: Disasters and development; disasters and gender; United Nations, Hyogo and Sendai Framework Action Plans
Hafta 11: What is SWOT analysis / risk analysis? How is it done?

Hafta 12: History of disaster management system in Turkey, lessons learned, new regulations; actors and their roles in disaster management (central and local governments, private sector, civil society organizations and citizens); disaster management structure, laws, regulations and institutions in Turkey; post-1999 and post-2023 earthquake assessments
Hafta 13: Examples of disaster management systems from Turkey and the world, comparison; economic dimensions and effects of disasters; risk transfers and disaster funds
Hafta 14: Monitoring changes in knowledge, awareness and development; course evaluation in terms of achievements

courses.name: Memory and Culture in New Media Ecology
courses.purpose: This course aims to focus on human memory through technological advances and the cultural transformations of contemporary society in digital networks. It is designed to embrace both theoretical arguments and narratives in the new media ecology through interdisciplinary perspectives that focus on the sociological, political, philosophical, ontological, and cultural trajectories of technology.
courses.description: This course discusses human memory through technological developments and the cultural transformations of contemporary society in digital networks. It embraces theoretical arguments and narratives in New Media Ecology and Critical Memory studies through interdisciplinary perspectives focused on the main debates and theoretical frameworks of digital society analysis. It defines and analyzes the cultural trajectories of technology as well as the fundamental epistemological, sociological, political, philosophical, and ontological assumptions underlying social research. The course examines the impact of digital culture and critically evaluates the role of technology in society and memory. It investigates how digital media challenges traditional concepts of identity, community, body, politics, and personal relationships.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to discuss the relationships between memory and the digital revolution.

Students will be able to analyze the digitalizing world by focusing on the sociological, political, philosophical, and cultural aspects of technology and networked popular culture in relation to memory and culture.
Students will understand specific concepts and terminology related to memory and culture in the New Media Ecology.

Students will be able to read and speak about specific topics related to the course content, such as artificial intelligence and hive minds, collective trauma, binding turns, cultural memory, cultural identity and ideologies, tangible and intangible memories, myths and digital narratives, media memory, hatred and forgiveness, healing discourses, and conflicting ideologies.
Students will be able to critically analyze and discuss memory and culture.

Students will be able to follow discussions on historical materialism, philosophy of history, the role of redemption, and peaceful discourse in digital media. They will develop their own perspectives on the analysis of the post-truth era and on presentism and cynical attitudes toward history.

Students will be able to discuss various topics related to the futuristic aspects of memory: astrobiology, transhumanism, posthumanism, cyborgs, anthropocentrism, neganthropocene, multi-planetary life, and cyberpunk.
Students will talk about anthropocentric life from anthropocentrism to posthumanism.
The course also explores the dark sides of digital media history narratives, focusing on Big Data, Data Mining, Data Management, Data Surveillance, and Dystopia.
Students can improve their critical reading skills with their own interpretations by focusing on the cultural archaeology of popular digital culture and discourses on digital media.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course introduction. What are the benefits and harms of the digital, networked, information age? Retrospective analysis of the cultural meaning of technology.

Hafta 2: What is media ecology? Introduction to memory studies. Collective memory and its main components and cultural identity. A critical view of the digital age and culture. Main debates. Digital storytelling, media and technological determinism: broad economic, political, and cultural transitions as far-reaching as the early 19th-century Industrial Revolution. The emergence of urban print culture in the 15th century, the changing roles of reader and writer in interactive digital texts, and the inherently collaborative nature of digital narratives. Algorithms, the future of artificial intelligence. Günther Anders: the role of technology, Heidegger's Gestell, Bernard Stiegler on techniques.

Hafta 3: Meta-level discussion of key terminology: hive mind, posthumanism, artificial intelligence, collective trauma, binding turns, myths, hatred, healing discourses, post-truth and conflicting ideologies, competitive memory, immanent subject, social media, hypermedia, post-memory, digital postmodernism, digital aesthetics, neuroscience, neuro-politics, neuropsychology, techno-communication, futurism, artificial consciousness, AI, VR, XR, MR, metaverse, transhumanism, posthumanism, cyborgs, anthropocentrism, neganthropocene, cyberpunk, big data, data mining, data management, dataveillance, dystopia.

Hafta 4: Assmann: individual, social and cultural memory; analysis of media memory (pdf); media memory: theories and methodologies; Halbwachs' thought; Henri Bergson's philosophy; the Annales school of social and intellectual history: historians Marc Bloch and Lucien Febvre; cultural memory and early civilization: writing, remembrance, and political imagination - Jan Assmann.
Hafta 5: Media memory, ethics and testimony, new media memory, memory and digital media: six dynamics of the global memory field.
Hafta 6: Media memory and popular culture, media memory, journalism and journalistic practice, journalism as a forward-looking memory tool, archive, media, trauma.
Hafta 8: Archive, media, trauma, student analysis of digital media discourses and presentation on memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 9: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 10: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 11: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 12: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 13: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 14: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 15: Evaluation of discussions on memory and culture in the context of New Media Ecology.

courses.name: Digital Photography
courses.purpose: This course focuses on photography with emphasis on its digital aspects. It is designed to introduce students to the fundamentals of digital camera photography, show the relationship between painting and photography, provide information about all stages of digital photography, and introduce various photography methods.

Students will learn about the brief history of photography, the basics of the digital camera, the fundamentals of photography, and digital photography processes. Main course materials will be uploaded to the Blackboard system, but students are advised to take their own notes during classes.

Understand the unique challenges of outdoor photography and capture striking landscapes and nature scenes
Develop street and candid photography skills and be aware of ethical considerations in public photography
Understand image resolution and file formats and adapt photography to various online platforms
Be aware of copyright and intellectual property issues in photography

courses.name: Fundamentals of Project Planning
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with information about national and international grant programs in the fields of education and research, enabling them to prepare projects for these grant opportunities.
courses.description: This course covers the basic principles, processes, and methods related to project design and planning, as well as fundamental information on preparing project budgets. The course content was prepared within the scope of the EU Jean Monnet Module project titled “EU Grants and Project Management,” which was awarded grant support under the coordination of the instructor.

Follow grant calls from reliable sources.

courses.name: Gender Studies
courses.purpose: The course aims to develop students’ knowledge of the theoretical foundations and key concepts in gender studies, to examine gender roles and discrimination in different contexts, to analyze the interaction between gender and other social factors, and to strengthen critical thinking skills for analyzing gender-related issues and their social impacts. Through this course, students will gain valuable insights into the complexities of gender dynamics in modern society and develop the ability to critically approach gender-related issues across various disciplines.
courses.description: This course offers a broad range of topics designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of gender studies and its contemporary relevance. The content includes gender concepts, roles, stereotypes, discrimination, feminism, violence, and the intersection of gender with various social domains such as media, education, family, work, and politics.
learning_outcomes: Define and explain the basic concepts and theories in gender studies.
Identify and analyze gender roles and stereotypes in various social contexts.
Understand the historical development of feminism and its different waves.
Recognize and critically analyze forms of gender-based violence and discrimination.
Evaluate gender representation in media and educational materials.
Analyze gender dynamics in family structures and interpersonal relationships.
Assess gender inequalities in the labor market and workplace.
Understand the challenges and importance of gender equality in political participation.
Develop and express informed perspectives on current gender issues.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: What is Gender? Theoretical Framework.
Hafta 12: Labor from a Gender Perspective

courses.name: Health Care in Multicultural Societies
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop the ability of health science students and health professionals to provide patient care in a culturally sensitive manner by explaining the effects of cultural differences on health care. It also encourages respect for cultural diversity while equipping students with the knowledge and skills needed for effective communication and patient care.
courses.description: This course examines health inequalities across different cultures, the causes of differences in health outcomes, and strategies to reduce these inequalities. It provides cultural sensitivity training to help students communicate effectively with patients from diverse cultural backgrounds. The course also addresses immigrants' access to health services, the health problems of immigrant communities, and health policies specific to these communities. In addition, it explores traditional medical practices in different cultures and their interaction with modern medicine, helping students understand and integrate different treatment approaches.
learning_outcomes: Understand health inequalities and identify the causes of differences in health outcomes across cultures.
Develop cultural sensitivity skills to facilitate effective communication with patients from different cultural backgrounds.
Understand the challenges and barriers faced by immigrant populations in accessing health services.
Recognize and analyze health problems specific to immigrant communities and understand their unique health needs.
Examine and integrate traditional medical practices from different cultures with modern medical approaches.
Promote the value of cultural diversity and encourage an attitude of respect and appreciation for cultural differences.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Cultural sensitivity and communication skills
Hafta 4: Effective communication practices and scenarios
Hafta 5: Health inequalities: causes and consequences
Hafta 6: The impact of cultural differences on health
Hafta 7: Access to health services for immigrants
Hafta 9: Health policies and strategies for immigrants
Hafta 10: Traditional medical practices and diversity
Hafta 11: The relationship between modern medicine and traditional medicine
Hafta 12: Integration of traditional and modern medical practices
Hafta 13: Evaluation of cultural diversity and conclusion
Hafta 14: Health problems of immigrant communities
resources: Bhopal, R. S. (2007). Ethnicity, race, and health in multicultural societies: foundations for better epidemiology, public health, and health care. Oxford University Press.
Ritter, L. A., & Hoffman, N. (2009). Multicultural health. Jones & Bartlett Publishers.

courses.name: Principles of Cryptocurrency
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental principles of cryptocurrency, including its technical foundations, usage areas, market structure, and future role in business and society.
courses.description: This course covers the definition and history of cryptocurrencies, their technical foundations, different cryptocurrency types, decentralized finance (DeFi) projects, usage areas, regulatory and legal issues, market analysis, portfolio management, risk factors, and future trends.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition and History of Cryptocurrencies
Hafta 8: Regulatory and Legal Issues of Cryptocurrencies
Hafta 10: Portfolio management and risk factors
Hafta 11: The Future of Cryptocurrencies and Integration with Business
resources: Lecture notes

courses.purpose: To increase knowledge and awareness about bioelectricity.
courses.description: Definition of bioelectricity, electric properties of living beings, examples from plants, animal cells, organs, bioelectricity coordinated cell functions, bioelectricity linked diseases and bioelectricity based diagnostic and therapeutic applications.
learning_outcomes: Explains what bioelectricity means.
Explains the effect of bioelectricity on biological functions.

Hafta 3: Processes that create bioelectricity - Ion channels

Hafta 6: Bioelectric component in biological functions-II

Hafta 11: Use of bioelectricity for therapeutic purposes
Hafta 13: General evaluation of the course

courses.name: Rising China: Power and Problems
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to analyze rising China and its influence in the regional and global order in terms of economic, military, and nuclear power, and to participate in discussions by being informed about current global issues.
courses.description: This course provides undergraduate students with a broad understanding of China’s political, economic, and strategic development in the modern era. The main discussions in the course begin with the lowest point in China’s history, when the country was transformed into a socialist state, and continue with China’s contemporary rise and its effects on the world. The course addresses questions such as: In what ways is China a rising country? How did this rise happen? How does China’s rise affect the United States and the global system?
learning_outcomes: Students will finish the course with basic knowledge about China Studies.
Students will gain critical thinking skills and a broad range of analytical tools to understand China’s rise and its global effects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The Founding of the People's Republic of China and the Socialist Order
Hafta 2: The New World Order after the Cold War and China’s Discourse of Multipolarity

Hafta 4: China’s Global Policies in the Age of Globalization
Hafta 5: China’s Areas of Economic Power
Hafta 6: China’s Military and Nuclear Potential
Hafta 10: China’s Taiwan Policy and Problems
Hafta 11: China’s Areas of Competition and Problems
Hafta 12: China’s Regional and Global Influence

courses.name: Pharmaceutical Aspects of Nutritional Disorders
courses.purpose: To identify the role of common vitamins in normal physiology and disease, examine the beneficial effects of vitamin supplementation, and determine the potential toxic effects of vitamin supplements.
courses.description: This course covers the central role of vitamins in nutrition. It introduces vitamin discoveries as factors that prevent specific diseases and discusses their importance in health support, often without the specificity that enabled their discovery.
learning_outcomes: Students are expected to acquire the following competencies: vitamins play a central role in nutrition. Vitamin discoveries as factors preventing specific diseases have marked the emergence of the field. They are also important for their roles in health support, which often lack the specificity that facilitated their discovery.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Perspectives on the Vitamin Concepts
Hafta 3: Considering the Individual Vitamins Water-soluble Vitamins- Vitamin C
Hafta 11: Using Current Knowledge of the Vitamins
resources: The Vitamins: Fundamental Aspects in Nutrition and Health. 4th edition 2012, Elsevier, UK.

courses.name: Management and Organization
courses.purpose: This course is designed to help students develop an understanding and awareness of the fundamentals of management and the behavior of organizations, provide a general overview of the field of management, and offer insights into the concept of organizational structure. By understanding how management theory has evolved and exploring the four basic functions of management—planning, organizing, leading, and controlling—students will learn how organizations use scarce resources to achieve their goals.
courses.description: This course covers basic management and business administration concepts; organisational environment and its variables, the stages of management in a historical process and its reflections to the present, managerial functions and current management practices.
learning_outcomes: Knows management and related concepts, can explain the relationships between them
Understand the evolutionary process of management theories and make connections
Explains the main components of the internal and external environments of business, their impact, importance and influence on an organisation.
Explains how decisions are made in an organisation, including the types of decisions to be made, problem definition and various decision-making approaches.
Understands management functions
Defines basic business concepts, principles and values that contribute to organisational performance.
Reveals the subsystems within the business and the relationships between them
Knows post-modern management concepts
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Ders Hakkında ve Temel Kavramlar
Hafta 2: Yönetim Teorileri: Kalsik Yönetim Teorisi
Hafta 3: Management Theories: Classical Approach (Scientific, Management Process, Bureaucracy)
Hafta 5: Neo-classical management approach: Behavioural Management Theory, Hawthorne studies, Kurt Levin Leadership Studies, C. Agyris- Mature and Immature Human Model,
Hafta 10: Yönetim Fonksiyonları: Yönlendirme ve Liderlik

courses.name: Decision Making and Problem Solving Techniques
courses.purpose: In this course, we will look at the decision-making process, paying close attention to the basic decision types, tools, methods, and insights to help you quickly learn how it involves both logic and emotion. When you complete the course, you will have practical tools to quickly determine the type of decision you are trying to make, the available tools and methods you may use, and the way to effectively engage your teams in the process.
courses.description: This course is an introduction to the decision-making process. It covers normative, prescriptive, and descriptive theories of judgment and choice; models of decision-making processes; the effects of context, experience, memory, and knowledge on decision-making; biases and heuristics that affect decision-making; and real-world applications of these topics.
learning_outcomes: Learn the basic concepts and terms of decision theory.
Learn to structure, model, and classify decision-making problems.
Learn how to deal with uncertainty and risk in decision-making.
Explain the qualitative limitations of managerial decision-making and suggest strategies to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of a decision.
Generate and evaluate alternative solutions.
Apply critical and creative thinking to decision-making processes.
Consider ethical issues in the decision-making process.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Orientation to the course / Introduction to Decision Making and Problem Solving
Hafta 2: Cognitive Foundations of Decision Making
Hafta 3: Rational and Intuitive Decision-Making Models (Classical Models and Bounded Rationality / Intuition in Problem Solving)
Hafta 4: Problem Identification and Structuring (Clearly Defining Problems / Tools for Structuring Complex Problems)
Hafta 5: Biases and Heuristics (Strategies for Reducing Bias in Decision Making)
Hafta 7: Creative Thinking and Innovation Tools
Hafta 8: Group Decision Making and Team Problem Solving / Selecting and Evaluating Solutions
Hafta 9: Quantitative Tools in Decision Making (Decision Trees, Payoff Matrix, SWOT Analysis, Cost-Benefit Analysis)
Hafta 10: Conflict Resolution and Negotiation in Decision Making
Hafta 12: Decision Making Under Uncertainty and Risk
Hafta 13: Integrating Decision Making and Problem Solving in Organizations
Hafta 14: Organizational Decision Making and Leadership

courses.name: Management in Different Cultures
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the knowledge and skills needed to manage, communicate, and lead effectively in different cultural settings, address ethical challenges, and develop strategies for global partnerships and assignments.
courses.description: This course provides an in-depth understanding of how cultural differences affect management practices and organizational behavior in a globalized world. Using case studies, discussions, and practical examples, it examines the key concepts of intercultural communication, cross-cultural management, and global leadership. Students will analyze cultural frameworks, management challenges in different cultural contexts, and develop effective decision-making and problem-solving strategies in diverse environments. The course emphasizes the importance of cultural awareness and adaptability in achieving organizational success in international settings.
learning_outcomes: Identify and analyze cultural environments and their effects on organizational practices.
Communicate effectively in intercultural settings.
Understand global leadership principles and adapt leadership styles to different cultural contexts.
Evaluate ethical dilemmas and social responsibilities in managing international organizations.
Develop strategies for successful partnerships and negotiations in diverse cultural environments.
Prepare for global assignments by creating management plans that address cultural challenges.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course, syllabus, and expectations. Introduction to Management in Different Cultures.
Hafta 2: Global Managers in a Changing World
Hafta 9: Management Ethics and Social Responsibility
Hafta 11: Global Teams and Global Assignments
Hafta 12: Critical Issues in Cross-Cultural Management
resources: Steers, R., M. 2020. Management Across Cultures, 4. Ed., Cambridge University Press.
Wildman, J. L., Griffith, R. L., Armon, B. K. 2016. Critical Issues in Cross Cultural Management, Springer.

courses.name: Consumer Behavior
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to examine consumer and consumer behavior literature and adapt culture to brand management strategies.
courses.description: This course introduces the basic concepts of consumer behavior and aims to help students understand the effects of culture and subcultures on individuals' purchasing behavior. The brand management process is examined in all its stages.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the effects of various factors on the consumer decision-making process with their outcomes.
Explain the consumer purchasing decision-making process.
Explain the basic concepts and models of consumer behavior.

courses.name: Innovative Strategies for New Product Development
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the skills to make informed decisions about which new products to develop and which strategies to use in the process. It builds a conceptual framework and connects it with real-life examples to provide a solid foundation for designing new product strategies and development processes.
courses.description: The course focuses on researching opportunities, developing strategies, and designing the processes of creating new products. It emphasizes the high-risk nature of new product development and aims to increase the chances of success for new products.
learning_outcomes: Understand the strategic importance of continuous innovation and new product/service development across different market areas and industries.
Develop an in-depth understanding of innovation and new product development processes.
Analyze market needs and opportunities and evaluate this information.
Apply processes to generate new product and service ideas.
Master the concepts and tools required for new product development through case studies and projects.
Appreciate the importance of design and design thinking in the product development process.
Gain the ability to evaluate and test new product concepts.
Develop strategies for bringing new products to market.
Design and develop their own new product or service and create a launch plan, gaining hands-on experience in the new product development process.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Students learn the fundamentals of identifying and selecting market opportunities, focusing on unmet needs and gaps across different sectors.
Hafta 2: The course continues with deeper analysis of potential market areas, developing students' ability to evaluate market needs and understand customer problems.
Hafta 3: Methods for creating product concepts suitable for identified opportunities are introduced, including brainstorming and idea development strategies.
Hafta 4: Students expand idea generation methods and develop initial product ideas, emphasizing alignment with market needs and business goals.
Hafta 5: Students learn to evaluate product concepts in terms of feasibility, market appeal, and strategic fit using techniques such as SWOT and cost-benefit analysis.
Hafta 6: Building on previous evaluation techniques, students focus on deeper assessment of product ideas and the importance of risk analysis and alignment with organizational goals.
Hafta 7: This week culminates the concept evaluation phase with an applied case study, allowing students to practice evaluation tools in a practical setting.
Hafta 8: Students present their projects showcasing product concepts developed through opportunity identification, concept creation, and evaluation.
Hafta 9: Project presentations continue, allowing more students to present their work and receive feedback.
Hafta 10: Students learn how to bring product concepts closer to reality through prototyping, testing, and using feedback for improvement.
Hafta 11: The course deepens the development phase by addressing user testing, design adjustments, and production planning.
Hafta 12: The course focuses on strategies for successfully launching a new product, including timing, positioning, and marketing tactics.
Hafta 13: The final section prepares students for the practicalities of introducing a new product, focusing on positioning and market entry strategies.
Hafta 14: Students present group projects demonstrating the full product development process from opportunity identification to launch.
resources: New Products Management, 12th ed., by Merle Crawford and Anthony Di Benedetto, McGraw-Hill

courses.name: Modern Leadership Theories
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of historical and contemporary leadership theories and their practical applications in modern organizations. By examining transformational, servant, authentic, adaptive, digital, creative, and other emerging leadership approaches, students develop analytical skills to evaluate leadership effectiveness across different cultural and organizational contexts. The course emphasizes experiential learning through case analyses, projects, and self-assessments, enabling students to critically assess their own leadership styles and acquire the competencies needed to lead ethically, inclusively, and innovatively in dynamic environments.
courses.description: This course examines the historical development of foundational leadership theories, contemporary leadership theories, and their practical applications in today’s dynamic and globalized business environment. It develops students’ critical understanding of leadership through theoretical foundations, self-assessments, real-world case analyses, and experiential projects.
learning_outcomes: Analyze and compare major modern leadership theories.
Apply leadership theories to organizational problems.
Evaluate leadership effectiveness in different cultural and situational contexts.
Demonstrate leadership competencies through team-based projects.
Reflect on their own leadership styles and development paths.
Reach original conclusions by using the interdisciplinary nature of the field.
Position the field of leadership within the broader area of social sciences and identify its interdisciplinary relations.
Follow developments in the field of leadership.
Develop strategies and plans for modern leadership styles.
Contribute to academic and applied studies in the field of leadership.
Define the ethical values of the field and be aware of issues such as the universality of social rights, social justice, quality, and cultural values.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and Definition of Leadership
Hafta 2: Evolution of Leadership Thought – Early Theories – Trait Theories (Great Man, Trait Theory)
Hafta 3: Classical Theories – Behavioral and Contingency Theories (Ohio & Michigan Schools, Lewin, Early Charismatic, Contingency, Path-Goal, LMX)
Hafta 5: Value- and Change-Oriented Theories (Transactional, Laissez-Faire, Servant, Transformational)
Hafta 7: New Generation Theories (Adaptive, Emotional Intelligence, Distributed/Shared, Authentic, Cross-Cultural, Ethical/Responsible, Complexity)
Hafta 8: The Difference Between Leadership and Management
Hafta 9: Leadership Styles in Different Sectors and Business Types
Hafta 10: New Leadership Approaches Reflecting the 21st Century: Workplace Trends (Creative, Digital, Inclusive Leadership)
Hafta 11: Leadership Role in Crisis Management, Case Studies
Hafta 12: Leadership in the Age of Artificial Intelligence (AI)
Hafta 14: How Have New Business Dynamics Changed Leadership Characteristics?

courses.name: Turkish Cinema
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand and interpret contemporary cinema by presenting the historical development of Turkish cinema and revealing the historical background and foundations of the films they watch today.
courses.description: This course examines the historical development of Turkish cinema from its emergence to the present, the stages it has gone through in relation to technological, social, and political developments, and the changes in cinematic language and narration through the study of various cinema movements.
learning_outcomes: Describe the general characteristics of Turkish cinema from its beginnings to the present.
Recall information about the beginnings of cinema in Turkey.
Recall information about the Theater People, Transition, and Cinematographers periods.
Recall the Yeşilçam period, film production processes, and the functioning of the industry.

courses.name: Journey to the Micro World of Cells!
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fascinating and diverse world of microorganisms and their important roles in nature and human life. Through a combination of theoretical knowledge and presentations, students will learn about microbial diversity and function, cellular structures and processes, microscopy and imaging techniques, the human microbiome, and the connections between the micro world and real-world issues. The course also aims to build foundational knowledge in microbiology and cellular biology while encouraging critical thinking for further study or careers in biological research, health, biotechnology, and environmental science.
courses.description: Introduction to the micro world, the importance of microorganisms in nature and human life, microscopy and imaging technologies, cell biology, prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, bacteria and archaea, viruses, fungi and protists, the human microbiome, and the roles of microorganisms in environmental processes such as biogeochemical cycles, waste management, and bioremediation. The course also includes student presentations.
learning_outcomes: Explains microbial diversity and subcellular structures, including bacteria, fungi, protists, and viruses, and their roles in ecosystems, human health, and disease.
Distinguishes between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, including their structures and functions, especially cellular organelles, membranes, and genetic material.
Identifies microscopy techniques and knows how to use light microscopes and other imaging tools to observe and analyze microorganisms, cellular structures, and biological samples.
Examines the human microbiome, especially the gut microbiome, and explains how it affects human health, disease, and treatment, including the roles of antibiotics and probiotics.
Evaluates real-world applications of microbiological concepts in areas such as environmental sustainability, biotechnology, public health, and epidemiology.
Works effectively in groups to research, discuss, and present findings on topics related to microbial biology, genetic engineering, and the microbiome.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the Micro World; Overview of the micro world; Importance of microorganisms in nature and human life; Introduction to microscopes and imaging technologies; Introduction to microscopy techniques; Microscopic observation of basic samples (onion cells, pond water)
Hafta 2: Cell Biology; Structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; Functions of cellular organelles; Cell membranes and transport mechanisms; Microscopic examination of plant and animal cells

Hafta 3: Bacteria and Archaea; Introduction to bacteria and archaea; Structure and function of bacterial cells; Role of bacteria in ecosystems (nitrogen fixation, decomposition); Exploring bacterial cultures on agar plates; Examining bacterial diversity with micrographs

Hafta 4: Viruses – Life at the Boundary; Structure of viruses; How viruses replicate: lytic and lysogenic cycles; Role of viruses in disease and genetic engineering; Discussing historical pandemics caused by viruses

Hafta 5: Fungi and Protists; Introduction to fungi: yeasts, molds, and mushrooms; Protists: diversity and habitats; Ecological importance of fungi and protists; Observing fungal growth and examining protists from water samples
Hafta 6: The Human Microbiome; Microorganisms living in and on the human body; Role of the gut microbiome in health and disease; Effects of antibiotics and probiotics

Hafta 7: Microorganisms and the Environment; Microbial roles in biogeochemical cycles (carbon, nitrogen, sulfur); Microbial decomposition and waste management; Bioremediation: using microorganisms to clean pollution
resources: Molecular Biology of the Cell by Bruce Alberts, Alexander D. Johnson, Julian Lewis, and Martin Raff
Microbiology: An Introduction by Gerard J. Tortora, Berdell R. Funke, and Christine L. Case
Principles of Virology by Jane Flint, Vincent D. Racaniello, Glenn F. Rall, and Anna Marie Skalka
The Human Microbiome: A New Frontier in Health and Disease by Julian R. Marchesi and Jacques Ravel

courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce digital media literacy by providing students with skills to access, evaluate, and create digital media content, while also developing their critical thinking on political, social, and legal issues that are widely discussed in the new media environment.
courses.description: This course covers the basic principles of digital media literacy, legal rights and digital surveillance, information literacy and digital manipulation, online politics, and big data.

Follows and critically approaches political, social, and legal issues widely discussed in the new media environment.

courses.purpose: To provide students with a comprehensive understanding of Turkey's business world and economic environment. To understand the local business culture
To be able to analyze economic indicators
To recognize sectors in Turkey and evaluate investment opportunities

courses.name: Global Economy and New Trends
courses.purpose: To provide an understanding of global issues and countries in light of historical, economic, commercial, political, social, geographical, religious backgrounds and current developments, disputes and cooperation among countries; to draw inferences about potential future markets and international trade topics; and to develop policy recommendations based on these inferences.
courses.description: This course examines world political and technology history, international competition, and global economic dynamics. It covers wars that have shaped the world, current disputes, areas of cooperation, the influence of religion, the developing African economy, and current international economic, political, social, and commercial developments, with an emphasis on analyzing international relations.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to analyze the effects of world political and technology history on global developments.
Students will be able to explain international competition and foreign trade theories and evaluate economic dynamics among countries.
Students will be able to interpret wars that shaped the world, current international disputes, and the effects of religion on trade and social life in a historical context.
Students will be able to analyze data on world trade volume and the developing African economy.
Students will gain insight into the effects of social media on daily life, the economy, and trade.

Hafta 3: The World Economy by Countries
Hafta 4: The World Economy by Countries
Hafta 5: Wars Shaping the World, Current Disputes

Migration and Social Order

Understand international migration theories
Understand the historical background of international migration
Evaluate the economic and social aspects of international migration
Discuss migration policies

Hafta 7: International Labor Migration and the Global Economy

courses.name: Global Climate Changes and Green Transformation
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the scientific foundations of climate change and the policy and economic mechanisms driving the transition to a low-carbon economy. It also seeks to build a basic understanding of international climate governance, carbon pricing, and green transformation policies.
courses.description: This course examines global climate change from scientific, economic, and policy perspectives. It covers the causes and impacts of climate change, market failures, and the transition to a low-carbon economy. The course also analyzes international climate governance, carbon pricing mechanisms, and emissions trading systems, with special emphasis on the European Green Deal, CBAM, and their effects on international trade and green transformation.
learning_outcomes: Explain the scientific foundations and main causes of global climate change.
Analyze the economic rationale of climate policies, including market failures and carbon pricing mechanisms.
Evaluate the effects of climate change on sectors, trade, and economic development.
Assess international climate agreements and climate governance frameworks.
Interpret the European Green Deal, CBAM, and emissions trading systems in the context of green transformation.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the Course; Course Syllabus
Hafta 2: Climate Science and the Global Climate

courses.name: European Politics
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide a broad perspective on the European Union and its place in the world. By the end of the course, students should be able to discuss contemporary European Union policies and evaluate current debates related to the European Union.
courses.description: This course explains the historical development, institutions, and structure of the European Union. It addresses the main issues shaping contemporary European politics, the role of Europe in the world, and the future of European integration.
learning_outcomes: Understand the historical development and evolution of the European Union
Evaluate the main debates in contemporary European politics
Analyze developments in contemporary European politics from a critical perspective
Develop a comprehensive understanding of the challenges and hopes facing European integration

courses.name: Sociology of Beliefs and Cross-Cultural Studies
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the complex relationships between belief systems and cultural structures from a sociological perspective. Students will examine how different societies form, interpret, and practice beliefs, and analyze the effects of cultural norms, values, and social structures on beliefs. Through cross-cultural analysis, they will develop a deeper understanding of the diversity and commonalities of belief systems around the world.
courses.description: This course examines the complex relationship between belief systems and cultural structures from a sociological perspective. Students will investigate how various societies form, interpret, and practice beliefs, and how cultural norms, values, and social structures influence them. Through cross-cultural analyses, the course aims to develop a comprehensive understanding of the diversity and commonalities of belief systems worldwide. Students will critically evaluate the role of beliefs in shaping individual identities and social dynamics through lectures, discussions, and case studies.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the interaction between belief systems and cultural structures.
Apply sociological theories to belief systems.
Make cross-cultural comparisons.
Evaluate the impact of beliefs on social stratification.
Examine the role of religion in contemporary society.
Develop cultural competence.
Demonstrate critical thinking and reflect on their own belief systems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the basic concepts of sociology and the sociological imagination.
Hafta 2: The relationship between culture and social structure.
Hafta 3: Sociological analysis of belief systems. Students are informed about the formation and continuity processes of belief systems.
Hafta 4: Social functions of religion and secularization processes.
Hafta 6: Identity and social belonging in a cultural context.
Hafta 9: The impact of belief systems on social change.
Hafta 10: Modernization, globalization, and cultural change.
Hafta 11: The relationship between belief systems and social inequalities.
Hafta 12: The impact of religion on political behavior and public culture.
Hafta 13: Social effects of religious institutions through case studies.
Hafta 14: General review of the topics discussed throughout the course.
Hafta 15: Final exam. Students are given 5 questions to answer at home and are expected to combine what they learned in class with their own life experiences.

Lecturer notes, powerpoint slides and videos

courses.name: Branding and Brand Supports
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the fundamental concepts, strategies, and applications of branding. It also seeks to develop the analytical and decision-making skills needed for successful brand management by covering topics such as brand identity, brand positioning, brand equity, brand communication, and consumer perception. In addition, students will gain an understanding of brand-building processes through national and international examples.
courses.description: Branding and the marketing mix, building strong brands, marketing and communication programs, measuring brand performance, brand audits, brand design and structures, managing brand equity for long-term success.
learning_outcomes: Learn relevant concepts
Understand product and brand management concepts
Comprehend strategies such as brand awareness and positioning in a global market environment
Understand sustainable brand management in a market environment where cultural differences are considered
Understand changing trends and technologies to improve brand management skills
Understand branding and relate concepts with example cases
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Definition of the brand, positioning, market segmentation, consumer behavior
Hafta 4: Brand loyalty, perceived quality, brand identity
Hafta 6: Brand equity and brand extension

courses.name: Pharmaceutical Aspects of Nutritional Disorders
courses.purpose: To identify the role of common vitamins in normal physiology and disease, examine the beneficial effects of vitamin supplements, and determine the potential toxic effects of vitamin supplementation.
courses.description: This course covers the central role of vitamins in nutrition, the historical significance of vitamin discoveries as factors preventing specific diseases, and their importance in health support due to their often nonspecific roles.

Vitamin discoveries as factors preventing specific diseases have marked the emergence of the field.
They are also important because of their roles in health support, which are often lacking in specificity.

Hafta 3: Considering the Individual Vitamins Water-soluble Vitamins- Vitamin C
Hafta 11: Using Current Knowledge of the Vitamins

courses.name: Health Promotion
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with basic knowledge about protecting and improving the health of individuals, families, and community groups, developing healthy lifestyle behaviors, and planning, implementing, and evaluating behavior change strategies.

Define the conditions and risk factors affecting health.
Explain the role and responsibilities of the midwife in health promotion.
Explain programs and practices aimed at health promotion.

courses.name: Extended Reality Technologies with XR Turkey
courses.purpose: This course aims to comprehensively examine the fundamental principles, application areas, and social impacts of extended reality (XR) technologies such as augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), and mixed reality (MR). In collaboration with XR Turkey, students will gain both theoretical knowledge and experience through current examples of sectoral practices. By the end of the course, students are expected to be able to analyze XR technologies creatively, evaluate them, and develop their own projects.
courses.description: This course covers the theoretical foundations and practical applications of augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), and mixed reality (MR) technologies in depth. It addresses the historical development of XR technologies, technical components, user experience design, and narrative structures, while focusing on usage examples in fields such as education, health, architecture, and culture. Within the scope of the course, students have the opportunity to listen to invited speakers from the sector and participate in workshops in collaboration with XR Turkey. Students are expected to develop a creative and technically strong XR project at the end of the term.
learning_outcomes: The student can explain the basic concepts and historical development of XR technologies.
The student can analyze XR applications and evaluate usage examples in different fields.
The student can develop creative and technical skills to design and present XR content at a basic level.
